skills.name: wcag-audit-team
skills.short_desc:
WHAT: 5-role WCAG 2.1 accessibility compliance audit (Perceivable + Operable + Understandable + Robust experts → Compliance Synthesizer) following W3C POUR principles.
Use when auditing public-facing websites for legal accessibility compliance (ADA, Section 508, EN 301 549) or inclusivity standards.
Do NOT use for internal admin-only tools, rapid prototypes, or non-web content (PDFs, native apps).
skills.detail_desc:
# WCAG 2.1 Accessibility Compliance Audit Team

A 5-role team following the B+C pattern (parallel decomposition → sequential integration) that audits web content against WCAG 2.1's four principles (Perceivable, Operable, Understandable, Robust). Each principle expert works in isolation to prevent cross-influence, then the Compliance Synthesizer integrates findings into a unified compliance verdict. This solves the single-agent failure mode where one reviewer cannot simultaneously maintain four distinct accessibility perspectives without converging to superficial checks.

## Workflow

0. **Pre-flight: check dependencies** — read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and verify available tools (python, curl, node are optional). Report missing items to user. The team can run on pure LLM WCAG knowledge if all skills/tools are missing. **User decides** whether to proceed.

1. **Context setup** — Leader parses target content (HTML/CSS/URL), defines audit scope (full page, specific components, entire site), and sets conformance level target (A, AA, or AAA). Prepare context package for each principle expert. See [bind.md](bind.md) § Resource Constraints for scope limits.

2. **Parallel principle audit** — Dispatch all four principle experts simultaneously (perceivable-expert, operable-expert, understandable-expert, robust-expert). Each expert audits their assigned WCAG principle in isolation. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 2 for detailed audit criteria and [roles/*.md](roles/) for each expert's methodology.

3. **Collect and validate** — Leader collects outputs from all experts, validates schema compliance. Retry malformed outputs per [bind.md](bind.md) § Failure Handling. Proceed with partial set if any expert fails after retry.

4. **Synthesis** — compliance-synthesizer integrates all findings, calculates overall conformance level, prioritizes by severity, identifies cross-principle patterns, and generates remediation roadmap. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 4 for synthesis logic.

5. **Final: emit WCAG Compliance Report** — Leader formats final report with audit metadata, conformance breakdown, findings by severity, cross-principle patterns, remediation roadmap, and overall verdict. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) § Final Report Format.

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| perceivable-expert | Audit Principle 1 (Perceivable) | Every run, parallel with other experts | Context package (target content, scope, conformance level) | none | [roles/perceivable-expert.md](roles/perceivable-expert.md) |
| operable-expert | Audit Principle 2 (Operable) | Every run, parallel with other experts | Context package | none | [roles/operable-expert.md](roles/operable-expert.md) |
| understandable-expert | Audit Principle 3 (Understandable) | Every run, parallel with other experts | Context package | none | [roles/understandable-expert.md](roles/understandable-expert.md) |
| robust-expert | Audit Principle 4 (Robust) | Every run, parallel with other experts | Context package | none | [roles/robust-expert.md](roles/robust-expert.md) |
| compliance-synthesizer | Integrate findings into compliance report | After all experts complete | All expert outputs + conformance target | none | [roles/compliance-synthesizer.md](roles/compliance-synthesizer.md) |

> Before dispatching each teammate, read the corresponding role file and extract the
> `## Inline Persona for Teammate` section — paste it directly into the dispatch prompt.
> Most adopting agents do NOT auto-load role files for teammates.

## Files

| File | What it contains | When to read |
|---|---|---|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Mermaid diagram, step-by-step audit protocol, integration rules, Final Report format | Before first dispatch — the complete playbook |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | Resource limits (15 min wall-clock, 250k tokens), behavioral constraints (principle isolation), failure handling and degraded modes | When hitting limits, handling failures, or needing degraded-mode rules |
| [roles/*.md](roles/) | Per-role identity (motto), success criteria, WCAG criteria focus, output schema, Inline Persona for Teammate | Before dispatching each expert — extract Inline Persona |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | External skills and tools (optional: python, curl, node) | **Startup** — verify deps, report missing items, user decides go/no-go |

skills.name: screenshot
skills.short_desc: 提供截图功能，与文档助手联合使用
skills.detail_desc:
## When to Use

Use when the task needs a screenshot of a desktop app, browser page, simulator, region, window, or full screen, especially for debugging, QA, documentation, release notes, bug reports, visual review, or before/after comparison.

This skill is about taking the right screenshot reliably, not about editing images after the fact.

## Tool Choice

| Context | Best default | Why |
|---------|--------------|-----|
| macOS desktop or window | `screencapture` | Built-in, reliable, supports silent, interactive, region, and window capture |
| iOS Simulator | `xcrun simctl io booted screenshot` | More reliable than generic desktop capture for simulator output |
| Linux Wayland | `grim` + `slurp` | X11 tools often fail or behave oddly on Wayland |
| Linux X11 / headless CI | `scrot` or browser-native capture | Works in minimal or virtual-display environments |
| Windows desktop capture | `nircmd savescreenshot` or Pillow `ImageGrab` | Easier than verbose PowerShell screen APIs |
| Web page or web app | Playwright | Best for stable viewport, element, full-page, masked, and regression screenshots |
| Visual diff / screenshot tests | Playwright with fixed viewport | Better control over animations, caret, masks, and reproducibility |

Default to the most native capture path first. Move to browser-native tooling when determinism, masking, element capture, or visual regression matters more than convenience.

## Core Rules

### 1. Pick the capture path by artifact, not by habit

- Desktop UI screenshots usually want OS-native tools.
- Web pages and web apps usually want browser-native capture, not a desktop screenshot of the browser window.
- Simulator screenshots should come from the simulator tooling when possible.
- Use region, window, or element capture when the point is local; use full screen or full page only when the full context matters.

### 2. Stabilize the target before capturing

- Dynamic pages should settle before capture: wait for network idle or the specific element that matters, then give fonts and transitions a brief moment to finish.
- Do not take the screenshot before the real rendered state exists.
- For browser capture, prefer explicit readiness over blind sleeps when possible.
- If the page never truly goes idle, wait for the exact UI state you need instead of chasing perfect stillness.

### 3. Freeze viewport, scale, zoom, and theme for reproducibility

- Screenshot comparisons are meaningless if viewport, zoom level, theme, or device scale changed.
- For browser captures, fix the viewport before taking baselines or before/after images.
- Retina and HiDPI displays can produce more pixels than expected; decide whether you want physical pixels or CSS-scale output and keep that choice consistent.
- If dark/light mode matters, capture both intentionally instead of mixing them accidentally.

### 4. Capture the smallest useful scope

- Element, region, or window screenshots are usually better than noisy full-screen captures.
- Full-page screenshots are useful for audits and archives, but long pages become hard to read and compare.
- For browser work, element screenshots or clipped regions usually produce cleaner diffs than full-page output.
- If the screenshot is evidence, keep enough surrounding context that the user can understand what they are looking at.

### 5. Remove noise before you capture

- Hide or avoid unstable UI when it is not the subject: cursors, carets, toasts, chat widgets, notifications, loading spinners, timestamps, and randomized content.
- Mask or avoid secrets, personal data, tokens, and internal URLs before capture.
- For Playwright-style browser capture, features like disabled animations, hidden carets, and masking are worth using when visual stability matters.
- If the noise is the bug, keep it; otherwise remove it.

### 6. Use the right output format

- PNG is the default for screenshots, UI, code, terminals, and text-heavy captures.
- JPEG is for photographic content, not normal screenshots.
- WebP is fine for sharing or storage when compatibility is acceptable, but do not default to it if the consumer expects plain PNG files.
- Avoid recompressing screenshots through JPEG pipelines unless the user explicitly wants smaller lossy output.

### 7. Make automation and CI captures debuggable

- On failures, save a screenshot immediately before retrying or moving on.
- Use stable filenames for baselines and timestamps for ad hoc or batch captures.
- In CI, identical viewport and deterministic state matter more than raw screenshot volume.
- Headless runs should prefer browser-native screenshots over trying to screen-grab the host display.

### 8. Validate that the screenshot is actually useful

- Check that the important detail is visible, legible, and not cropped away.
- Verify that secrets are not still visible in tabs, sidebars, URLs, notifications, or test data.
- Before/after comparisons should use the same viewport, zoom, theme, and state.
- A screenshot is bad if it is technically correct but useless for the human who needs it.

## High-Value Patterns

- macOS: `screencapture -x out.png` for silent capture, `-i` for interactive selection, `-R x,y,w,h` for a fixed region.
- iOS Simulator: `xcrun simctl io booted screenshot out.png`
- Linux Wayland: `grim -g "$(slurp)" out.png`
- Playwright page capture: wait for the target state, then use page, element, clipped, or full-page screenshots deliberately.
- Playwright stability features worth remembering: fixed viewport, disabled animations, hidden caret, masks for sensitive regions, and stable theme/media settings.

## Common Traps

- Taking a browser-window screenshot when an element or page screenshot was the real need.
- Capturing before fonts, data, or layout transitions finish.
- Comparing screenshots with different viewport sizes or zoom levels and treating the diff as meaningful.
- Using JPEG for screenshots and blurring text, edges, and code.
- Letting timestamps, cursor blinks, notifications, or random data ruin visual diffs.
- Forgetting that Wayland breaks familiar X11 screenshot tools.
- Sharing screenshots with secrets still visible in tabs, sidebars, URLs, or test accounts.
- Taking full-page captures of huge pages and ending up with unreadable evidence.

## Related Skills
Install with `clawhub install <slug>` if user confirms:

- `playwright` — Browser automation, DOM interaction, and web screenshots
- `image` — Post-capture format, cropping, compression, and export decisions
- `image-edit` — Annotation, cleanup, masking, and targeted edits after capture
- `documentation` — Turning screenshots into docs, guides, and release assets
- `video` — When a flow should be recorded instead of reduced to still images

## Feedback

- If useful: `clawhub star screenshot`
- Stay updated: `clawhub sync`

skills.name: video-account-live-team
skills.short_desc:

不适用于仅搬运抖音内容。
skills.detail_desc:
# Video Account Live Team（视频号直播策划团队）

B+C混合模式团队：四个角色并行分析各自专业维度（选题、货盘、场控、私域），Leader整合协调后输出完整直播策划方案。解决单代理在视频号直播策划中容易忽略微信生态独特性（社交裂变机制、私域沉淀路径、视频号技术限制）的问题。

## Workflow

0. **Pre-flight: 检查依赖与收集上下文** — 读取 [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) 验证依赖状态，收集用户需求的完整上下文（品牌/人设、产品类型、直播目标、私域现状、技术条件）。用户决定是否继续（缺失依赖时）。

1. **并行分析: 四角色同时执行** — topic-selector、product-planner、live-controller、private-domain-coordinator 并行分析各自专业维度，输出结构化报告。详见 [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 1。

2. **整合协调: Leader汇总与冲突识别** — Leader汇总四份报告核心结论，识别跨维度冲突（选题-货盘匹配、场控-私域节奏等），评估整体方案可行性。详见 [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 2。

3. **冲突协调: 迭代调整（如有冲突）** — Leader向相关角色发送调整请求，角色更新报告，最多2轮迭代。无法协调的冲突标记[需用户决策]。详见 [bind.md](bind.md) § Failure Handling。

4. **Final: 输出直播策划全案** — 按Final Report Format组装完整方案，包含选题、货盘、场控、私域、协调要点、执行检查清单、时间线、预期效果。详见 [workflow.md](workflow.md) § Final Report Format。

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| topic-selector | 视频号选题策划 | 每次运行，Step 1并行 | 用户需求 + 品牌/人设背景 | 无（基于Inline Persona方法论） | [roles/topic-selector.md](roles/topic-selector.md) |
| product-planner | 视频号货盘规划 | 每次运行，Step 1并行 | 用户需求 + 品牌/人设背景 + 选题信息 | 无（基于Inline Persona方法论） | [roles/product-planner.md](roles/product-planner.md) |
| live-controller | 视频号场控设计 | 每次运行，Step 1并行 | 用户需求 + 品牌/人设背景 + 选题/货盘信息 | 无（基于Inline Persona方法论） | [roles/live-controller.md](roles/live-controller.md) |
| private-domain-coordinator | 视频号私域联动 | 每次运行，Step 1并行 | 用户需求 + 品牌/人设背景 + 选题/货盘/场控信息 | 无（基于Inline Persona方法论） | [roles/private-domain-coordinator.md](roles/private-domain-coordinator.md) |

> Before dispatching each teammate, read the corresponding role file and extract the
> `## Inline Persona for Teammate` section — paste it directly into the dispatch prompt.
> Most adopting agents do NOT auto-load role files for teammates.

## Files

| File | What it contains | When to read |
|---|---|---|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Mermaid流程图、详细步骤、质量门控、Final Report Format | 首次调度前 — 完整执行协议 |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | 资源约束、行为约束、失败处理、降级模式、冲突协调规则 | 遇到限制、处理失败、需要降级规则时 |
| [roles/*.md](roles/) | 各角色Identity、Success Criteria、Boundary、Output Schema、Inline Persona | 调度每个角色前 — 提取Inline Persona |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | 外部技能和工具依赖声明 | **启动时** — 验证依赖，报告缺失项，用户决定是否继续 |

skills.name: nasa-safe-code-rater
skills.short_desc: Score C/C++ code against NASA safe coding guidance using 10 normalized rules, produce a 0-100 safety score, and return an English report with findings, exclusions, and remediation. Use when the user invokes $nasa-safe-code-rater or asks to audit a repository/current file/diff for NASA-style safe C coding compliance.
skills.detail_desc:
# NASA Safe Code Rater

Use this skill to evaluate C/C++ code with a NASA-inspired 10-rule model and generate a scored report.

## Inputs

Accept optional user arguments:
- `target=repo|file|diff` (default `repo`)
- `path=<file-or-dir>` (required for `target=file`)
- `include_cpp=true|false` (default `true`)

If arguments are absent, run repository-wide analysis.

## Required Workflow

1. Resolve scope from user input.
2. Analyze only C/C++ files by default (`.c`, `.h`, `.cpp`, `.hpp`).
3. List non-C/C++ files as excluded items.
4. Evaluate all 10 rules in `references/nasa_rules.md`.
5. Produce an English markdown report containing:
- Executive summary
- Overall score and risk class (`Good`, `Warning`, `Critical`)
- Rule-by-rule findings table
- Top 5 remediation actions
- Insufficient evidence section

## Execution Commands

Use these scripts from the skill directory:

```bash
python3 scripts/analyze_repo.py --root <repo-path> --mode repo --json-out /tmp/nasa_analysis.json
python3 scripts/score_report.py --analysis /tmp/nasa_analysis.json --format markdown
```

For single file mode:

```bash
python3 scripts/analyze_repo.py --root <repo-path> --mode file --path <file-path> --json-out /tmp/nasa_analysis.json
```

For diff mode:

```bash
python3 scripts/analyze_repo.py --root <repo-path> --mode diff --json-out /tmp/nasa_analysis.json
```

To refresh normalized rules from a source PDF:

```bash
python3 scripts/extract_nasa_rules.py --pdf <path-to-pdf> --out references/nasa_rules.md
```

## Quality Gates

Require for critical findings:
- At least one concrete evidence line per failed rule.
- Rule IDs traceable to `references/nasa_rules.md`.

If no C/C++ files are in scope, return `not applicable` with reason and next step.

skills.name: anti-money-laundering-team
skills.short_desc: 3角色并行AML审计团队，包含模式识别专家、风险评级专家、合规检查专家。可识别可疑交易模式、评级客户风险、验证KYC完整性，适用于金融机构交易审查和KYC合规验证。
skills.detail_desc:
# 反洗钱合规审计团队

本团队采用并行分解模式，由模式识别、风险评级和合规检查三个角色独立分析金融交易的反洗钱合规状态。

## Workflow

0. **Pre-flight: check dependencies** — read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and verify all items are available.

1. **Leader: gather context** — collect transaction records, customer profiles, KYC documents, and regulatory requirements.

2. **Parallel AML audit** — dispatch all three roles simultaneously:
   - **pattern-recognizer**: identifies suspicious patterns, detects ML typologies
   - **risk-rater**: rates risk levels, evaluates geographic/sector factors
   - **compliance-checker**: verifies KYC completeness, validates procedures

3. **Leader: integrate findings** — collect all outputs, compose by domain slice, identify cross-domain alerts.

4. **Final: emit AML Compliance Report** — comprehensive report with suspicious patterns, risk ratings, and compliance gaps.

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| pattern-recognizer | Identifies suspicious patterns | Every run, parallel | Transaction records | [roles/pattern-recognizer.md](roles/pattern-recognizer.md) |
| risk-rater | Rates risk levels | Every run, parallel | Customer profiles, geographic data | [roles/risk-rater.md](roles/risk-rater.md) |
| compliance-checker | Verifies KYC completeness | Every run, parallel | KYC documents, procedures | [roles/compliance-checker.md](roles/compliance-checker.md) |

## Files

| File | What it contains | When to read |

| [roles/*.md](roles/) | Per-role identity, Inline Persona | Before dispatching |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | External dependencies | Startup |

skills.name: gdpr-compliance-team
skills.short_desc: 3角色对抗式GDPR审计团队，包含法律专家、数据工程师、风险评审员。可审计国际系统的欧盟数据隐私合规状态，识别法律、技术、风险三维度合规缺口，生成认证准备报告。
skills.detail_desc:
# GDPR Compliance Audit Team

This team performs adversarial GDPR compliance audits using three independent perspectives: legal interpretation, technical implementation verification, and risk quantification. The adversarial pattern ensures blind spots are surfaced—each role critiques the system without seeing others' findings, preventing convergence on a single narrative.

## Workflow

0. **Pre-flight: check dependencies** — read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and verify all items are available. Report results to the user:
   - **All passed** → proceed to step 1.
   - **Some missing** → list each missing item with its `purpose` field, distinguish `required: true` (will likely fail) vs `required: false` (degraded). **Ask the user whether to proceed or abort.**

1. **Leader: gather context** — collect target system documentation, data flow diagrams, privacy policies, and any existing compliance reports. Distribute identical context to all three roles.

2. **Parallel adversarial audit** — dispatch all three roles simultaneously (they MUST NOT see each other's outputs):
   - **legal-specialist**: maps data flows to GDPR articles, identifies legal gaps
   - **data-engineer**: validates technical privacy controls, checks encryption/access
   - **risk-critic**: quantifies risks, challenges assumptions, prioritizes findings

3. **Leader: integrate findings** — collect all role outputs, surface contradictions verbatim (do NOT mediate), categorize by severity.

4. **Final: emit GDPR Compliance Report** — structured report with critical gaps, recommendations, and surfaced contradictions for human resolution.

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| legal-specialist | Interprets GDPR articles, identifies legal gaps | Every run, parallel with other roles | System docs, data flows, privacy policies | None | [roles/legal-specialist.md](roles/legal-specialist.md) |
| data-engineer | Validates technical privacy controls | Every run, parallel with other roles | System architecture, data processing specs | None | [roles/data-engineer.md](roles/data-engineer.md) |
| risk-critic | Quantifies risks, challenges assumptions | Every run, parallel with other roles | All context + outputs from legal/data roles (after they complete) | None | [roles/risk-critic.md](roles/risk-critic.md) |

> Before dispatching each teammate, read the corresponding role file and extract the `## Inline Persona for Teammate` section — paste it directly into the dispatch prompt. Most adopting agents do NOT auto-load role files for teammates.

## Files

| File | What it contains | When to read |
|---|---|---|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Mermaid diagram, step-by-step protocol, integration rules, Final Report format | Before first dispatch — the complete playbook |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | Resource limits, behavioral constraints, failure handling and degraded modes | When hitting limits, handling failures, or needing degraded-mode rules |
| [roles/*.md](roles/) | Per-role identity, success criteria, output schema, Inline Persona for Teammate | Before dispatching each teammate — extract Inline Persona |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | External skills and tools required to run | **Startup** — verify deps, report missing items, user decides go/no-go |

skills.name: audio-quality-team
skills.short_desc:
音频质量审计组。审核底噪、动态范围与母带平衡。
适用于播客、有声书发布前的最终质量关。
不适用于过程中的临时检查。
skills.detail_desc:
# audio-quality-team

音频质量审计组。在播客、有声书发布前进行最终质量把关，通过三个独立视角交叉审核，确保音频达到发布标准。

## Workflow

0. **预检：检查依赖** — 读取 [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) 并验证。
   报告缺失项：`required: true` = 没有它很可能失败；`required: false` = 功能降级但仍可用。**由用户决定**是否继续。
   本团队无外部依赖 — 所有角色基于内联角色知识运行。

1. **并行审计阶段** — Leader 同时分发噪声审计师、动态范围分析师、母带平衡评审员。三个角色独立审计，互不干扰。详见 [workflow.md](workflow.md) Stage 1。

2. **Leader 整合阶段** — Leader 整合三个角色的审计报告，交叉验证发现的问题，标注一致性和冲突。详见 [workflow.md](workflow.md) Stage 2。

3. **Final: 输出质量报告** — Leader 输出完整质量审计报告、问题清单、修复建议和发布建议。详见 [workflow.md](workflow.md) § Final Report Format。

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| noise-auditor | 审核底噪和背景噪声 | Stage 1，并行执行 | `{AUDIO_FILE}`, `{AUDIO_METADATA}`, `{CONTENT_TYPE}` | none | [roles/noise-auditor.md](roles/noise-auditor.md) |
| dynamic-range-analyst | 分析动态范围和响度 | Stage 1，并行执行 | `{AUDIO_FILE}`, `{AUDIO_METADATA}`, `{TARGET_LOUDNESS}` | none | [roles/dynamic-range-analyst.md](roles/dynamic-range-analyst.md) |
| master-balance-reviewer | 评审母带平衡和音质 | Stage 1，并行执行 | `{AUDIO_FILE}`, `{AUDIO_METADATA}`, `{GENRE_STANDARD}` | none | [roles/master-balance-reviewer.md](roles/master-balance-reviewer.md) |

> 在分发每个队友之前，读取对应的角色文件并提取
> `## Inline Persona for Teammate` 章节 — 直接粘贴到分发提示词中。
> 大多数采用代理不会自动加载角色文件。

## Files

| File | What it contains | When to read |
|---|---|---|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Mermaid 图、详细步骤、整合规则、最终报告格式 | 首次分发前 — 完整执行手册 |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | 资源限制、行为约束、失败处理和降级模式 | 遇到限制、处理失败或需要降级模式规则时 |
| [roles/*.md](roles/) | 每个角色的身份、成功标准、输出 Schema、Inline Persona | 分发每个队友前 — 提取 Inline Persona |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | 运行所需的外部技能和工具 | **启动时** — 验证依赖，报告缺失项，用户决定是否继续 |

## WHAT

对音频文件进行发布前的最终质量审计，包括：
- **底噪审核**：背景噪声识别、噪声类型分类、严重程度评估
- **动态分析**：动态范围测量、压缩程度评估、响度一致性检查
- **母带评审**：频率平衡分析、声道平衡检查、整体音质评估

## WHEN

适用于：
- 播客发布前的最终质量关
- 有声书上线前的质量审核
- 音频课程发布前的质量检查
- 任何需要达到发布标准的音频内容

## NOT

不适用于：
- 录音过程中的临时检查（应使用实时监控工具）
- 混音阶段的中间检查（应使用混音师的专业判断）
- 简单的音量调整需求（无需团队审计）

## 灵感来源

AES 音频质量评估标准：https://www.aes.org/

## 使用方式

```markdown
请使用 audio-quality-team 对以下音频进行发布前质量审计：

[音频文件信息]
- 文件路径/URL: [音频位置]
- 内容类型: [播客/有声书/课程]
- 时长: [分钟数]
- 目标响度: [如 -16 LUFS for 播客]

[可选补充]
- 录音环境: [专业录音室/家庭录音/户外]
- 后期处理: [已做哪些处理]
- 发布平台: [Apple Podcasts/喜马拉雅/等]
```

## 输出示例

```markdown
## 音频质量审计报告

### 底噪审核结果
- **底噪水平**: -52 dB (轻微，可接受)
- **噪声类型**: 空调低频噪声 + 电脑风扇高频噪声
- **严重程度**: ⚠ 轻微 — 建议在安静段落使用降噪处理
- **Verdict**: NOISE-MODERATE

### 动态范围分析结果
- **动态范围**: 18 dB (适中)
- **响度**: -15.8 LUFS (符合播客标准)
- **压缩程度**: 轻度压缩，保留自然动态
- **响度一致性**: ✅ 各段落响度波动 < 2 LU
- **Verdict**: DYNAMIC-STRONG

### 母带平衡评审结果
- **频率平衡**: ✅ 人声频段清晰，低频不浑浊
- **声道平衡**: ✅ 单声道内容，无相位问题
- **整体音质**: 清晰自然，适合发布
- **Verdict**: MASTER-STRONG

### 交叉验证
- ✅ 三角色审计结果一致：整体质量良好
- ⚠ 噪声审计师发现的问题在安静段落明显，建议处理

### 问题清单
1. [轻微] 00:15:30-00:18:00 安静段落底噪明显
2. [建议] 00:05:00 处有一处呼吸声过大

### 发布建议
✅ **建议发布** — 整体质量达标，仅需对标注段落做轻微优化（可选）
```

skills.name: patent-strategy-team
skills.display_name: 专利布局策略团队技能
skills.short_desc:
采用 4 角色协作完成专利策略分析：技术拆解、检索映射、风险研判与布局整合。
用于评估技术可专利性、规避侵权风险和申请优先级。
不替代执业律师出具的正式法律意见。
skills.detail_desc:
# 专利策略团队

该团队以“职责分离 + 质量门控”的方式协同完成复杂研究任务，避免单角色在关键判断上同时当运动员和裁判。每个角色只对本阶段产出负责，由 Leader 最终整合为可执行决策包。

## Workflow

0. **前置检查：验证依赖** — 读取 [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml)，确认依赖可用并标注缺失影响。
1. **invention-decomposer**：拆解核心发明点并定义保护边界。
2. **patent-mapper**：构建关键词与分类号检索映射。
3. **freedom-risk-analyst**：识别潜在侵权风险与规避路径。
4. **portfolio-planner**：给出申请组合与时间优先级建议。
5. **Leader 组装最终交付** — 输出 **专利布局策略包**，包含结论、证据、风险与行动项。

skills.detail_desc:
# Labor Arbitration Preparation Team

This team uses a sequential pipeline pattern to prepare for labor arbitration hearings: strategy development → evidence preparation → mock evaluation.

## Workflow

0. **Pre-flight: check dependencies** — read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and verify all items.

1. **Stage 1: Strategy Development** — arbitration-strategist develops case strategy, identifies key arguments, plans hearing approach. Quality gate: strategy covers all dispute issues.

2. **Stage 2: Evidence Preparation** — evidence-curator receives strategy output, organizes evidence, identifies gaps, prepares submission strategy. Quality gate: evidence covers all key arguments.

3. **Stage 3: Mock Evaluation** — mock-arbitrator receives strategy + evidence outputs, simulates tribunal perspective, evaluates argument strength. Quality gate: mock verdict documented.

4. **Final: emit Arbitration Preparation Report** — comprehensive report with strategy, evidence, and mock evaluation.

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| arbitration-strategist | Develops arbitration strategy | Stage 1, sequential | Dispute details, labor law context | [roles/arbitration-strategist.md](roles/arbitration-strategist.md) |
| evidence-curator | Organizes and validates evidence | Stage 2, after strategist | Strategy output + evidence materials | [roles/evidence-curator.md](roles/evidence-curator.md) |
| mock-arbitrator | Simulates tribunal perspective | Stage 3, after curator | Strategy + Evidence outputs | [roles/mock-arbitrator.md](roles/mock-arbitrator.md) |

## Files

| File | What it contains | When to read |
|---|---|---|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Mermaid diagram, protocol, Final Report | Before first dispatch |

| [roles/*.md](roles/) | Per-role identity, Inline Persona | Before dispatching |

skills.name: business-process-reengineering-team
skills.short_desc:
业务流程重构团队，通过五阶段专业化流水线（诊断 → 利益分析 → 设计 → 实施 → 评估）识别流程冗余和瓶颈，设计全新数字化流转路径。
使用场景：企业面临严重的官僚效率低下、跨部门审批极慢、流程环节冗余明显。
不适用于：简单的办公流程微调、局部效率优化、不涉及数字化重构的流程调整。
skills.detail_desc:
# 业务流程再造团队

这是一个 **Pattern C（专业化流水线）** 团队技能。价值来自严格的阶段纪律和质量门控：每个角色专注自己的领域，阶段输出必须通过门控才能进入下一阶段，防止低质量输出污染后续分析。团队解决的单角色失败模式是：流程再造需要多维度专业分析（技术诊断、组织重构、方案设计、实施规划、效果验证），单角色难以同时胜任所有阶段且容易跳过关键步骤。

## Workflow

0. **预检查：依赖检查 + 上下文收集** — Leader 读取 [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) 验证依赖。本团队依赖 3 个社区技能（mermaid-diagrams、project-manager、data-visualization），均为 `required: false`（增强输出质量，非必需）。收集流程上下文。用户确认流程信息完整后方可启动。详见 [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 0。

1. **Stage 1: 流程诊断** — process-diagnostician 执行。输入流程上下文和案例样本，识别冗余环节和瓶颈节点，输出健康度评分。若 mermaid-diagrams 可用，可生成时间-活动分布图。门控：诊断结论为 GO 且报告完整。详见 [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 1。

2. **Stage 2: 利益相关者分析** — stakeholder-analyst 执行。依赖 Stage 1 输出，绘制利益相关者地图，识别阻力来源和应对策略。门控：分析结论为 GO-阻力可控。详见 [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 2。

3. **Stage 3: 流程再造设计** — reengineering-designer 执行。依赖 Stage 1+2 输出，设计新流程蓝图，对比改进幅度。若 mermaid-diagrams 可用，可生成新流程 Mermaid 图。门控：设计结论为 GO 且环节减少 ≥50%。详见 [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 3。

4. **Stage 4: 实施路径规划** — change-implementation-advisor 执行。依赖 Stage 2+3 输出，制定阶段计划、试点方案、回退机制。若 project-manager 可用，可生成结构化里程碑模板。门控：实施结论为 GO 且包含回退机制。详见 [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 4。

5. **Stage 5: 效果评估框架设计** — performance-evaluator 执行。依赖 Stage 1+3+4 输出，定义 KPI、监控机制、因果关系验证方法。若 data-visualization 可用，可生成 KPI 趋势图模板。门控：评估结论为 GO 且包含基线对比。详见 [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 5。

6. **最终：输出完整报告** — Leader 整合 5 个阶段输出，按层级组织（问题诊断 → 重构阻力 → 设计方案 → 实施路径 → 效果验证）。详见 [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 6 Final Report Format。

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| process-diagnostician | 诊断流程冗余和瓶颈 | Stage 1（首个角色） | 流程上下文、案例样本 | mermaid-diagrams (社区) | [roles/process-diagnostician.md](roles/process-diagnostician.md) |
| stakeholder-analyst | 分析重构阻力来源 | Stage 2（诊断通过后） | 诊断报告、组织架构 | 无前置依赖 | [roles/stakeholder-analyst.md](roles/stakeholder-analyst.md) |
| reengineering-designer | 设计全新流程蓝图 | Stage 3（利益分析通过后） | 诊断报告、利益分析报告 | mermaid-diagrams (社区) | [roles/reengineering-designer.md](roles/reengineering-designer.md) |
| change-implementation-advisor | 规划实施路径和风险预案 | Stage 4（设计通过后） | 设计方案、利益分析报告 | project-manager (社区) | [roles/change-implementation-advisor.md](roles/change-implementation-advisor.md) |
| performance-evaluator | 定义 KPI 和监控机制 | Stage 5（实施计划通过后） | 设计方案、实施计划、诊断报告 | data-visualization (社区) | [roles/performance-evaluator.md](roles/performance-evaluator.md) |

> 分派每个队友前，读取对应角色文件并提取 `## Inline Persona for Teammate` 部分 —— 直接粘贴到分派提示中。大多数采用框架不会为队友自动加载角色文件。

## Files

| File | What it contains | When to read |
|---|---|---|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Mermaid 流水线图、分步协议、门控定义、最终报告格式 | 首次分派前 — 完整执行手册 |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | 资源限制、行为约束、失败处理和降级模式 | 遇到限制、处理失败或需要降级规则时 |
| [roles/*.md](roles/) | 角色身份、成功标准、输出 Schema、Inline Persona | 分派每个队友前 — 提取 Inline Persona |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | 外部技能和工具依赖 | **启动时** — 验证依赖，报告缺失项，用户决定继续与否 |

skills.short_desc: 3角色流水线税务筹划团队，包含政策解读专家、风险评估专家、方案设计专家。可解读税法政策、量化合规风险、设计合法优化方案，适用于重大重组、跨境交易、大额资产配置场景。
skills.detail_desc:
# 税务合规筹划团队

本团队采用专业化流水线模式，按顺序执行政策解读、风险评估和方案设计三个阶段。每个阶段有明确的质量门控，确保方案在合规前提下实现税务优化。

## Workflow

0. **Pre-flight: check dependencies** — read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and verify all items are available. Report results to the user:
   - **All passed** → proceed to step 1.
   - **Some missing** → list each missing item with its `purpose` field, distinguish `required: true` (will likely fail) vs `required: false` (degraded). **Ask the user whether to proceed or abort.**

1. **Stage 1: Policy Interpretation** — policy-interpreter analyzes transaction structure, identifies applicable tax regulations, and maps to legal frameworks. Quality gate: all transaction components mapped to specific tax articles.

2. **Stage 2: Risk Assessment** — risk-assessor receives policy interpretation output, quantifies compliance risks, evaluates audit probability, and identifies red flags. Quality gate: risk matrix complete with severity ratings.

3. **Stage 3: Solution Design** — solution-architect receives both prior outputs, designs compliant optimization solutions, and validates against risk constraints. Quality gate: solution passes risk threshold check.

4. **Final: emit Tax Planning Report** — comprehensive report with policy analysis, risk assessment, and optimization recommendations.

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| policy-interpreter | Interprets tax regulations, maps transactions to legal frameworks | Stage 1, sequential | Transaction structure, business context | None | [roles/policy-interpreter.md](roles/policy-interpreter.md) |
| risk-assessor | Quantifies risks, evaluates audit probability, identifies red flags | Stage 2, after policy-interpreter | Policy interpretation output | None | [roles/risk-assessor.md](roles/risk-assessor.md) |
| solution-architect | Designs compliant optimization solutions | Stage 3, after risk-assessor | Policy + Risk outputs | None | [roles/solution-architect.md](roles/solution-architect.md) |

> Before dispatching each teammate, read the corresponding role file and extract the `## Inline Persona for Teammate` section — paste it directly into the dispatch prompt. Most adopting agents do NOT auto-load role files for teammates.

| [roles/*.md](roles/) | Per-role identity, success criteria, output schema, Inline Persona for Teammate | Before dispatching each teammate — extract Inline Persona |

skills.detail_desc:
# livestream-director-team

直播场控专家组，三角色协同把控电商、知识分享直播全过程。脚本逻辑员设计内容节奏，互动策略师规划观众参与，应急专家保障直播安全。

## Workflow

0. **Pre-check: 验证依赖** — 读取 [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) 并验证。
   报告缺失项：`required: true` = 无此依赖可能失败；`required: false` = 降级但仍可用。**用户决定**是否继续。
   本团队无外部依赖 — 所有角色基于内联角色知识运行。

1. **并行规划阶段** — Leader 同时派发脚本逻辑员、互动策略师、应急专家。三角色独立工作，互不干扰。见 [workflow.md](workflow.md) Stage 1。

2. **Leader 整合阶段** — Leader 整合三角色输出，交叉验证一致性，标注一致点和冲突点。见 [workflow.md](workflow.md) Stage 2。

3. **Final: 输出直播场控方案** — Leader 输出完整直播场控方案，包含脚本逻辑、互动策略、应急预案。见 [workflow.md](workflow.md) § Final Report Format。

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| script-logic-officer | 设计直播脚本逻辑 | Stage 1, 并行执行 | `{LIVESTREAM_TYPE}`, `{PRODUCT_LIST}`, `{DURATION}` | none | [roles/script-logic-officer.md](roles/script-logic-officer.md) |
| interaction-strategist | 规划互动策略 | Stage 1, 并行执行 | `{LIVESTREAM_TYPE}`, `{TARGET_AUDIENCE}`, `{PROMOTION_GOALS}` | none | [roles/interaction-strategist.md](roles/interaction-strategist.md) |
| emergency-expert | 制定应急预案 | Stage 1, 并行执行 | `{LIVESTREAM_TYPE}`, `{RISK_HISTORY}`, `{PLATFORM_RULES}` | none | [roles/emergency-expert.md](roles/emergency-expert.md) |

> 派发每个队友前，读取对应角色文件并提取
> `## Inline Persona for Teammate` 部分 — 直接粘贴到派发提示词中。
> 大多数采用代理不会自动加载角色文件。

## Files

| File | What it contains | When to read |
|---|---|---|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Mermaid 图、详细步骤、整合规则、最终报告格式 | 首次派发前 — 完整执行手册 |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | 资源限制、行为约束、失败处理、降级模式 | 遇到限制、处理失败或需要降级规则时 |
| [roles/*.md](roles/) | 每个角色的身份、成功标准、输出格式、内联人设 | 派发每个队友前 — 提取 Inline Persona |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | 必需的外部技能和工具 | **启动时** — 验证依赖，报告缺失，用户决定 |

## WHAT

设计直播场控方案，包含：
- **脚本逻辑**：直播流程、时间节点、产品展示顺序、内容节奏
- **互动策略**：观众引导、弹幕响应、促销节奏、粉丝转化
- **应急预案**：风险预判、突发处理、平台合规、技术备份

## WHEN

适用于：
- 电商直播（带货、促销、新品发布）
- 知识分享直播（课程、讲座、问答）
- 企业直播（发布会、培训、活动）
- 有明确目标和时间规划的直播

## NOT

不适用于：
- 随手直播（无准备的日常分享）
- 纯聊天直播（无明确内容目标）
- 游戏直播（娱乐性质，无脚本需求）
- 简单通知类直播（无复杂流程）

## Inspiration

电商直播 SOP 操作手册：https://www.shopify.com/blog/live-shopping

参考框架：
- **直播前**：脚本设计、互动预热、风险排查
- **直播中**：节奏把控、观众互动、应急响应
- **直播后**：复盘分析、数据整理、优化建议

## Usage

```markdown
请使用 livestream-director-team 设计以下直播场控方案：

[直播信息]
- 直播类型：[电商带货/知识分享/企业活动]
- 直播时长：[预计时长]
- 产品/内容：[主要展示内容]
- 目标观众：[受众画像]

[可选详情]
- 平台规则：[抖音/快手/淘宝/微信等]
- 促销目标：[销售额/转化率/粉丝增长]
- 历史风险：[过往直播遇到的问题]
```

## Output Example

```markdown
## 直播场控方案

### 脚本逻辑设计
**直播流程**
| 时间段 | 内容模块 | 时长 | 关键动作 |
|--------|----------|------|----------|
| 0-5分钟 | 开场预热 | 5min | 问候、预告、抽奖启动 |
| 5-15分钟 | 产品A介绍 | 10min | 展示、痛点、优惠公布 |
| 15-20分钟 | 互动环节 | 5min | 问答、弹幕抽奖 |
| 20-35分钟 | 产品B介绍 | 15min | 深度讲解、对比演示 |
| 35-40分钟 | 促销高潮 | 5min | 限时优惠、库存告急 |
| 40-45分钟 | 收尾转化 | 5min | 总结、关注引导、下期预告 |

### 互动策略规划
**观众引导路径**
- 新观众：欢迎话术 → 内容预告 → 关注引导
- 活跃观众：弹幕互动 → 抽奖参与 → 转化引导
- 购买观众：下单确认 → 好评引导 → 复购提示

**促销节奏设计**
- 开场抽奖：激活观众，提升停留
- 中场问答：维持热度，筛选意向
- 收尾限时：制造紧迫感，促成转化

### 应急预案
**风险预判**
| 风险类型 | 触发条件 | 应对措施 |
|----------|----------|----------|
| 技术故障 | 画面卡顿/声音中断 | 切换备用设备，主播话术安抚 |
| 弹幕攻击 | 恶意评论刷屏 | 开启评论过滤，引导正面话题 |
| 产品争议 | 价格/质量质疑 | 准备证据素材，主播专业回应 |
| 平台违规 | 触发敏感词 | 立即纠正，备选话术替换 |

### Cross-Validation
- ✅ 脚本时间节点与互动节奏匹配
- ✅ 应急预案覆盖脚本关键节点
- ⚠ 建议：产品B环节增加备用互动方案

### 执行检查清单
- [ ] 脚本时间节点确认
- [ ] 互动工具准备（抽奖/问答）
- [ ] 应急预案演练
- [ ] 平台规则复核
```

skills.name: corporate-ethics-team
skills.short_desc: 2角色对抗式伦理审计团队，包含ESG框架设计师、合规监察员。可审计企业治理伦理合规状态，设计ESG框架对标联合国全球契约，识别治理缺口和道德风险。
skills.detail_desc:
# Corporate Ethics Audit Team

This team performs adversarial corporate ethics audits using two independent perspectives: ESG framework design and ethical compliance monitoring. The adversarial pattern ensures governance blind spots are surfaced—each role critiques the organization without seeing the other's findings.

## Workflow

0. **Pre-flight: check dependencies** — read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and verify all items are available. Report results to the user:
   - **All passed** → proceed to step 1.
   - **Some missing** → list each missing item with its `purpose` field, distinguish `required: true` (will likely fail) vs `required: false` (degraded). **Ask the user whether to proceed or abort.**

1. **Leader: gather context** — collect corporate governance documents, existing ESG policies, supplier agreements, and industry standards. Distribute identical context to both roles.

2. **Parallel adversarial audit** — dispatch both roles simultaneously (they MUST NOT see each other's outputs):
   - **esg-standards-designer**: designs ESG framework aligned with global standards
   - **ethical-compliance-monitor**: monitors compliance, identifies violations, challenges assumptions

3. **Leader: integrate findings** — collect both role outputs, surface contradictions verbatim (do NOT mediate), categorize by governance domain.

4. **Final: emit Corporate Ethics Report** — structured report with governance gaps, ethical risks, and surfaced contradictions for human resolution.

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| esg-standards-designer | Designs ESG frameworks aligned with global standards | Every run, parallel with monitor | Governance docs, ESG policies, industry standards | None | [roles/esg-standards-designer.md](roles/esg-standards-designer.md) |
| ethical-compliance-monitor | Monitors compliance, identifies violations, challenges assumptions | Every run, parallel with designer | Governance docs, supplier agreements, compliance records | None | [roles/ethical-compliance-monitor.md](roles/ethical-compliance-monitor.md) |

> Before dispatching each teammate, read the corresponding role file and extract the `## Inline Persona for Teammate` section — paste it directly into the dispatch prompt. Most adopting agents do NOT auto-load role files for teammates.

## Files

| File | What it contains | When to read |
|---|---|---|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Mermaid diagram, step-by-step protocol, integration rules, Final Report format | Before first dispatch — the complete playbook |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | Resource limits, behavioral constraints, failure handling and degraded modes | When hitting limits, handling failures, or needing degraded-mode rules |
| [roles/*.md](roles/) | Per-role identity, success criteria, output schema, Inline Persona for Teammate | Before dispatching each teammate — extract Inline Persona |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | External skills and tools required to run | **Startup** — verify deps, report missing items, user decides go/no-go |

skills.name: annual-report-team
skills.short_desc:
WHAT: 4-role annual report production team (Data Synthesizer + Narrative Storyteller + Visual Designer + Executive Reviewer) that transforms raw performance data into executive-ready reports via B+C mixed pattern.
WHEN: Summarizing yearly performance for executives, board meetings, or investor calls.
NOT: Monthly updates, quarterly snapshots, or operational dashboards.
skills.detail_desc:
# Annual Report Team

A 4-role team that produces high-impact annual reports for executives. Uses a **B+C mixed pattern**: parallel decomposition for data synthesis and narrative development (B), followed by a specialization pipeline for visual design and executive review (C). Solves the failure mode where single-agent reports lack both data rigor and narrative appeal — executives get either dry numbers or fluffy prose, never both.

## Workflow

0. **Pre-flight: check dependencies** — read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and verify.
   Report missing items: `required: true` = likely fails without it; `required: false` = degraded but functional. **User decides** whether to proceed.
   The team can run on inline-persona-only mode if all skills are missing.
   Note: dependencies were populated during authoring via Stage 2 auto-matching (local scan) and optional post-generation community enrichment. Each entry's `source` field indicates origin (`local` or community URL).

1. **Parallel: Data Synthesis + Narrative Development** — data-synthesizer and narrative-storyteller dispatched in parallel.
   data-synthesizer receives annual data inputs and produces structured data summary with verdict.
   narrative-storyteller receives company context and strategic priorities, then data-synthesizer output via Leader relay, and produces narrative structure.
   See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 1 for quality gates and retry logic. See [bind.md](bind.md) for parallel isolation rules.

2. **Pipeline: Visual Design Specification** — visual-designer receives integrated outputs from Step 1.
   Produces visual hierarchy plan, chart specifications, layout structure, and accessibility checklist.
   See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 2 for quality gates. See [bind.md](bind.md) for C-pattern handoff discipline.

3. **Pipeline: Executive Review** — executive-reviewer receives all upstream outputs.
   Evaluates against 5 executive criteria, identifies strengths, deficiencies, unanswered questions, and provides verdict (APPROVE / REVISE / REJECT).
   See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 3 for quality gates and kick-back routing. See [bind.md](bind.md) for escalation rules.

4. **Final: emit Annual Report Package** — Leader integrates all outputs into cohesive report package.
   Contains: Executive Summary, Data Foundation, Narrative Structure, Visual Design Specification, Executive Review Summary, Production Readiness.
   Leader routes based on verdict enums, never interprets prose. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) for Final Report format.

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| data-synthesizer | Collect and synthesize annual performance data | Every run, parallel with narrative-storyteller | Annual data inputs, reporting period, prior year data | python, jq, curl (optional) | [roles/data-synthesizer.md](roles/data-synthesizer.md) |
| narrative-storyteller | Transform data into compelling narrative | Every run, parallel with data-synthesizer (receives data output via Leader relay) | Company context, strategic priorities, data-synthesizer output | None | [roles/narrative-storyteller.md](roles/narrative-storyteller.md) |
| visual-designer | Design visual hierarchy and chart specs | Every run, after Step 1 completion | Data summary, narrative structure, brand guidelines | ppt-generation, visualization-expert (community) | [roles/visual-designer.md](roles/visual-designer.md) |
| executive-reviewer | Evaluate executive-readiness | Every run, after Step 2 completion | All upstream outputs, executive priorities | None | [roles/executive-reviewer.md](roles/executive-reviewer.md) |

> Before dispatching each teammate, read the corresponding role file and extract the
> `## Inline Persona for Teammate` section — paste it directly into the dispatch prompt.
> Most adopting agents do NOT auto-load role files for teammates.

## Files

| File | What it contains | When to read |
|---|---|---|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Mermaid diagram, step-by-step protocol, integration rules, Final Report format | Before first dispatch — the complete playbook |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | Resource limits, behavioral constraints, failure handling and degraded modes | When hitting limits, handling failures, or needing degraded-mode rules |
| [roles/*.md](roles/) | Per-role identity, success criteria, output schema, Inline Persona for Teammate | Before dispatching each teammate — extract Inline Persona |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | External skills and tools required to run | **Startup** — verify deps, report missing items, user decides go/no-go |

skills.name: energy-management
skills.display_name: 能量管理和疲劳对抗团队技能
skills.short_desc:
一个3角色的能量管理和疲劳对抗系统，通过能量专家+睡眠教练+代谢优化师的协作，帮助用户克服慢性疲劳、稳定能量水平、提高日常精力。
使用场景：用户经常感到疲劳、下午能量崩溃、想提高能量水平、或想对抗慢性疲劳。
不适用于：医学疲劳综合征（ME/CFS）的医学诊断和治疗。
skills.detail_desc:
# 能量管理和疲劳对抗 (Energy Management)

这是一个三角色的能量管理系统，通过能量科学、睡眠优化和代谢改善的协作，帮助用户克服疲劳并稳定能量水平。该系统解决了"多喝咖啡"的短期解决方案导致的能量崩溃问题。

## Workflow

0. **前置检查：验证依赖** — 确认能量科学、睡眠优化和代谢健康技能可用。

1. **能量评估** — Leader 收集用户的疲劳模式、睡眠质量、生活方式、饮食等。

2. **并行任务**：
   - **Energy Expert** 评估能量问题根源，推荐策略
   - **Sleep Coach** 优化睡眠质量和恢复
   - **Metabolism Optimizer** 优化代谢和营养以稳定能量

3. **集成与优化** — 整合输出，创建完整的能量管理计划。

4. **最终交付** — 输出：能量诊断、睡眠优化计划、代谢优化方案、能量追踪。

## Roles

| id | Purpose | 何时分派 | 输入 | 关键依赖 | Role文件 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| energy-expert | 评估能量问题 | 首次 | 疲劳模式、症状 | energy-science | [roles/energy-expert.md](roles/energy-expert.md) |
| sleep-coach | 优化睡眠 | 并行 | 睡眠质量、习惯 | sleep-science | [roles/sleep-coach.md](roles/sleep-coach.md) |
| metabolism-optimizer | 代谢优化 | 并行 | 饮食、活动 | metabolic-health | [roles/metabolism-optimizer.md](roles/metabolism-optimizer.md) |

## Files

| 文件 | 内容 |
|---|---|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | 能量管理工作流 |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | 约束和警告 |
| [roles/*.md](roles/) | 三个角色的说明 |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | 依赖项 |
| [reference/](reference/) | 能量和代谢科学 |

skills.name: china-cross-border-ecommerce-launch-team
skills.display_name: 跨境电商启动团队技能
skills.short_desc:
WHAT: 跨境电商启动团队（平台选择、物流、税务、合规）。
Use when 中国卖家出海。
Do NOT use for 只做店铺装修。
skills.detail_desc:
# 跨境电商启动团队

一个三阶段专业化流水线团队，专注于中国卖家跨境电商出海启动。每个角色负责一个阶段：平台选择、物流方案、合规保障。此模式解决了单一角色模糊阶段边界、导致出海准备不充分的问题。

## Workflow

0. **Pre-flight: check dependencies** — read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and verify.
   Report missing items: `required: true` = likely fails without it; `required: false` = degraded but functional. **User decides** whether to proceed.
   The team can run on inline-persona-only mode if all skills are missing.

1. **Stage 1: 平台选择** — platform-analyst / input: seller_resources, target_markets, product_categories / action: 分析平台特性, 评估入驻门槛, 制定平台策略. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) for full step.

2. **Stage 2: 物流方案** — logistics-coordinator / input: platform_choice, product_specs, target_regions / action: 设计物流方案, 优化成本时效, 建立仓储体系. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) for full step.

3. **Stage 3: 合规保障** — compliance-specialist / input: logistics_plan, business_model, target_countries / action: 处理税务合规, 海关申报, 知识产权保护. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) for full step.

4. **Final: emit 跨境电商启动报告** — Leader integrates all outputs. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) for report format.

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| platform-analyst | 分析平台特性，制定平台选择策略 | Stage 1 (first) | seller_resources, target_markets, product_categories | none | [roles/platform-analyst.md](roles/platform-analyst.md) |
| logistics-coordinator | 设计跨境物流方案，优化成本时效 | Stage 2 (after Stage 1 gate passes) | platform_choice, product_specs, target_regions | platform-analyst output | [roles/logistics-coordinator.md](roles/logistics-coordinator.md) |
| compliance-specialist | 处理税务合规、海关申报、知识产权保护 | Stage 3 (after Stage 2 gate passes) | logistics_plan, business_model, target_countries | platform-analyst + logistics-coordinator outputs | [roles/compliance-specialist.md](roles/compliance-specialist.md) |

> Before dispatching each teammate, read the corresponding role file and extract the
> `## Inline Persona for Teammate` section — paste it directly into the dispatch prompt.
> Most adopting agents do NOT auto-load role files for teammates.

## Files

| File | What it contains | When to read |
|---|---|---|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Mermaid diagram, step-by-step protocol, integration rules, Final Report format | Before first dispatch — the complete playbook |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | Resource limits, behavioral constraints, failure handling and degraded modes | When hitting limits, handling failures, or needing degraded-mode rules |
| [roles/*.md](roles/) | Per-role identity, success criteria, output schema, Inline Persona for Teammate | Before dispatching each teammate — extract Inline Persona |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | External skills and tools required to run | **Startup** — verify deps, report missing items, user decides go/no-go |

skills.name: project-portfolio-manager-team
skills.short_desc: 项目组合管理专家组。负责跨项目资源竞争调配、风险平衡与战略优先级排序。用于 PMO 同时管理数十个关联项目、资源严重过载时。不适用于单个项目经理的日常汇报。
skills.detail_desc:
# Project Portfolio Manager Team

A-pattern team: 三角色并行独立审查项目组合，从资源、风险、战略三个维度提供对抗性分析。

## Why This Teamskill

项目组合管理面临三个独立的盲点风险：

1. **资源盲点**: PMO 往往只看项目需求总量，忽略时间维度的资源冲突和关键资源瓶颈
2. **风险盲点**: 单项目风险评估忽略组合层面的风险聚合效应（依赖链、风险集中度）
3. **战略盲点**: 高 ROI 项目可能偏离战略方向，但单一视角难以识别

三个角色从不同视角独立审查，Leader 整合但不仲裁矛盾，确保每个维度的专业判断不被稀释。

## Workflow

| Step | Executor | Action |
|---|---|---|
| 0 | Leader | Pre-flight check: 验证输入完整性 |
| 1 | All roles (parallel) | 独立分析：资源冲突 / 组合风险 / 战略对齐 |
| 2 | Leader | 收集输出，检查完整性 |
| 3 | Leader | 整合分析，生成健康度仪表盘 |
| 4 | Leader | 输出最终报告，保留矛盾 |

详见 [workflow.md](workflow.md)。

## Roles

| id | Purpose | Role file |
|---|---|---|
| resource-balancer | 识别资源冲突、计算利用率、提出分配建议 | [roles/resource-balancer.md](roles/resource-balancer.md) |
| risk-analyzer | 分析组合层面风险、依赖链风险、风险集中度 | [roles/risk-analyzer.md](roles/risk-analyzer.md) |

skills.name: script-optimize-team
skills.short_desc:
WHAT: 3-role specialization pipeline (Structure Architect + Dialogue Specialist + Pacing Editor) that diagnoses and refines scripts using Save the Cat! methodology.
WHEN: Refining scripts for film, YouTube series, or ads — when structure, dialogue, or pacing feel "off" despite good individual scenes.
NOT: Writing prose fiction, generating new scripts from scratch, or non-script creative writing.
skills.detail_desc:
# Script Optimization Team

A 3-stage specialization pipeline that enforces discipline across structural analysis, dialogue optimization, and pacing refinement. Each stage sees prior stage output but cannot rewrite it — preventing the common failure mode where one reviewer's suggestions bleed into another's domain. Inspired by Blake Snyder's Save the Cat! screenplay methodology.

## Workflow

0. **Pre-flight: check dependencies** — read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and verify.
   Report missing items: `required: true` = likely fails without it; `required: false` = degraded but functional. **User decides** whether to proceed.
   The team can run on inline-persona-only mode if all skills are missing.
   Note: dependencies were populated during authoring via Stage 2 auto-matching (local scan) and optional post-generation community enrichment. Each entry's `source` field indicates origin (`local` or community URL).

1. **Stage 1: Structure Analysis** — structure-architect receives full script + script type + target duration. Analyzes against Save the Cat! 15-beat framework. Output: Beat Map + Structural Issues + Recommendations + Verdict. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 1 for gate criteria.

2. **Stage 2: Dialogue Optimization** — dialogue-specialist receives script + structure-architect output. Analyzes subtext, character voice, conflict per scene. Output: Dialogue Analysis + Issues + Recommendations + Verdict. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 2 for gate criteria.

3. **Stage 3: Pacing Optimization** — pacing-editor receives script + prior stage outputs. Maps tension curve, identifies rhythm issues. Output: Tension Curve + Pacing Issues + Recommendations + Verdict. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 3 for gate criteria.

4. **Final: emit Optimization Report** — Leader integrates all stage outputs into unified report. Each stage's recommendations preserved in its section; verdicts preserved verbatim. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 4 for Final Report format.

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| structure-architect | Diagnose structural weaknesses using 15-beat framework | Stage 1 (first) | script content, script type, target duration | none | [roles/structure-architect.md](roles/structure-architect.md) |
| dialogue-specialist | Optimize dialogue for subtext, voice, conflict | Stage 2 (after structure gate passes) | script + structure-architect output | none | [roles/dialogue-specialist.md](roles/dialogue-specialist.md) |
| pacing-editor | Adjust tension curve and scene duration | Stage 3 (after dialogue gate passes) | script + structure + dialogue outputs | none | [roles/pacing-editor.md](roles/pacing-editor.md) |

> Before dispatching each teammate, read the corresponding role file and extract the
> `## Inline Persona for Teammate` section — paste it directly into the dispatch prompt.
> Most adopting agents do NOT auto-load role files for teammates.

## Files

| File | What it contains | When to read |
|---|---|---|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Mermaid diagram, step-by-step protocol, integration rules, Final Report format | Before first dispatch — the complete playbook |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | Resource limits, behavioral constraints, failure handling and degraded modes | When hitting limits, handling failures, or needing degraded-mode rules |
| [roles/*.md](roles/) | Per-role identity, success criteria, output schema, Inline Persona for Teammate | Before dispatching each teammate — extract Inline Persona |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | External skills and tools required to run | **Startup** — verify deps, report missing items, user decides go/no-go |

skills.name: knowledge-base-team
skills.short_desc:
Knowledge base structuring team with Information Architect, Content Curator, and Taxonomy Specialist working in parallel.
Use when structuring messy documents into a professional Wiki.
Do NOT use for personal note-taking or simple folder organization.
skills.detail_desc:
# Knowledge Base Team

This is a **B-pattern (Parallel decomposition)** team that solves the single-agent blind spot of "one perspective on structure." Three specialists analyze the document collection simultaneously from different angles: structure, content quality, and taxonomy. Leader integrates all outputs into a cohesive Wiki proposal.

**ISO 30401 Alignment**: This teamskill applies ISO 30401 knowledge management principles across all three roles:
- Information Architect maps the knowledge lifecycle (capture → organize → store → share → apply)
- Content Curator assesses knowledge quality dimensions (accuracy, completeness, currency, consistency)
- Taxonomy Specialist designs classification frameworks for knowledge retrieval and reuse

## Workflow

0. **Pre-flight: check dependencies** — read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and verify.
   Report missing items: `required: true` = likely fails without it; `required: false` = degraded but functional. **User decides** whether to proceed.

1. **Parallel Analysis** — Three roles run simultaneously:
   - information-architect: designs hierarchical structure and navigation pathways using ISO 30401 knowledge lifecycle mapping
   - content-curator: reviews content quality across ISO 30401 quality dimensions and identifies enhancement needs
   - taxonomy-specialist: designs faceted taxonomy and cross-reference system using semantic relationships
   See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 1 for details.

2. **Integration** — Leader combines outputs, resolves low-impact conflicts, surfaces high-impact conflicts for user decision.
   See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 2 for conflict resolution rules.

3. **Final: emit Wiki Structure Proposal** — Leader integrates all outputs into a cohesive proposal.
   Report includes structure, content assessment, taxonomy, implementation checklist, and ISO 30401 alignment summary. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 3.

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| information-architect | Design hierarchical structure and navigation using ISO 30401 lifecycle mapping | Every run (parallel) | Document collection + requirements | None (optional: wiki_query for existing Wiki) | [roles/information-architect.md](roles/information-architect.md) |
| content-curator | Review content quality across ISO 30401 dimensions and propose enhancements | Every run (parallel) | Document collection + structure (optional) | None (optional: wiki_lint for existing Wiki) | [roles/content-curator.md](roles/content-curator.md) |
| taxonomy-specialist | Design faceted taxonomy and cross-references using semantic relationships | Every run (parallel) | Document collection + existing tags (optional) | None (optional: wiki_query for existing Wiki) | [roles/taxonomy-specialist.md](roles/taxonomy-specialist.md) |

> Before dispatching each teammate, read the corresponding role file and extract the
> `## Inline Persona for Teammate` section — paste it directly into the dispatch prompt.
> Most adopting agents do NOT auto-load role files for teammates.

## Files

| File | What it contains | When to read |
|---|---|---|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Mermaid diagram, parallel analysis steps, integration rules, conflict resolution, Final Report format | Before first dispatch — the complete playbook |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | Resource limits, behavioral constraints, conflict resolution rules, failure handling and degraded modes | When hitting limits, handling failures, or needing degraded-mode rules |
| [roles/*.md](roles/) | Per-role identity, success criteria, ISO 30401 methodology, output schema, Inline Persona for Teammate | Before dispatching each teammate — extract Inline Persona |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | External skills and tools required to run (wiki_ingest, wiki_query, wiki_lint as optional) | **Startup** — verify deps, report missing items, user decides go/no-go |

skills.detail_desc:
# Cloud Topology Optimization Team

A 4-role Mixed A+B pattern team that audits cloud infrastructure across Cost, Performance, and Compliance pillars following AWS Well-Architected Framework methodology. Three parallel pillar experts analyze independently (isolation prevents convergence), then a Risk Synthesizer integrates findings and surfaces cross-pillar conflicts where cost savings become security disasters.

## Workflow

0. **Pre-flight: check dependencies** — read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and verify.
   Report missing items: `required: true` = likely fails without it; `required: false` = degraded but functional. **User decides** whether to proceed.
   The team can run on inline-persona-only mode if all skills are missing.
   Note: dependencies were populated during authoring via Stage 2 auto-matching (local scan) and optional post-generation community enrichment. Each entry's `source` field indicates origin (`local` or community URL).

1. **Parallel pillar analysis** — Leader dispatches cost-optimizer, performance-optimizer, compliance-auditor simultaneously.
   Each role receives cloud topology description and optional metrics/config data.
   Each role independently analyzes their pillar domain using AWS Well-Architected methodology.
   See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 1 for quality gates (≥3 findings per role).

2. **Cross-pillar synthesis** — Leader dispatches risk-synthesizer after all Step 1 outputs collected.
   Risk Synthesizer receives all three pillar outputs, identifies cross-pillar interactions, prioritizes by business impact.
   See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 2 for quality gates (≥2 cross-pillar interactions).

3. **Final: emit Well-Architected Audit Report** — Leader compiles all findings into unified report.
   Surface cross-pillar conflicts verbatim — do NOT mediate. Resolution is the human user's call.
   See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 3 for Final Report format.

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| cost-optimizer | Identify cost waste with quantified savings | Step 1 (parallel with performance/compliance) | Cloud topology, utilization metrics, billing summary | aws-cost-operations | [roles/cost-optimizer.md](roles/cost-optimizer.md) |
| performance-optimizer | Identify performance bottlenecks with measurable impact | Step 1 (parallel with cost/compliance) | Cloud topology, performance metrics, scaling policies | aws-cloud-watch | [roles/performance-optimizer.md](roles/performance-optimizer.md) |
| compliance-auditor | Identify security/compliance gaps with severity classification | Step 1 (parallel with cost/performance) | Cloud topology, security config, compliance requirements | aws-security-scanner | [roles/compliance-auditor.md](roles/compliance-auditor.md) |
| risk-synthesizer | Synthesize findings into unified risk assessment | Step 2 (after Step 1 complete) | All three pillar outputs, business context | none | [roles/risk-synthesizer.md](roles/risk-synthesizer.md) |

skills.name: virtual-avatar-stylist-team

Use when 设计 VRChat/VTuber 的高保真数字形象。
Do NOT use for 2D 漫画人物创作。
skills.detail_desc:
# virtual-avatar-stylist-team

虚拟偶像/化身形象组。由骨架架构师、材质师、造型师组成的三人专家团队，专门负责设计 VRChat/VTuber 的高保真数字形象，确保虚拟形象在技术性能与视觉表现上达到专业水准。

## Workflow

0. **Pre-check: Validate Dependencies** — Read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and validate.
   Report missing items: `required: true` = may fail without this; `required: false` = degraded but functional. **User decides** whether to continue.
   This team has no external dependencies — all roles operate on inline role knowledge.

1. **Parallel Review Stage** — Leader dispatches Skeleton Architect, Material Artist, and Styling Designer simultaneously. Each role works independently. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Stage 1.

2. **Leader Integration Stage** — Leader integrates all three outputs, cross-validates skeleton-material-styling alignment, marks agreements and conflicts. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Stage 2.

3. **Final: Output Virtual Avatar Design Report** — Leader outputs comprehensive design report with skeleton structure, material specifications, and styling design. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) § Final Report Format.

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| skeleton-architect | 设计骨架结构 | Stage 1, parallel | `{BODY_TYPE}`, `{MOTION_RANGE}`, `{EXPRESSION_COUNT}` | none | [roles/skeleton-architect.md](roles/skeleton-architect.md) |
| material-artist | 设计材质贴图 | Stage 1, parallel | `{SKIN_TYPE}`, `{CLOTHING_MATERIAL}`, `{LIGHTING_ENV}` | none | [roles/material-artist.md](roles/material-artist.md) |
| styling-designer | 设计整体造型 | Stage 1, parallel | `{STYLE_REFERENCE}`, `{PERSONALITY}`, `{PLATFORM}` | none | [roles/styling-designer.md](roles/styling-designer.md) |

> Before dispatching each teammate, read the corresponding role file and extract
> the `## Inline Persona for Teammate` section — paste it directly into the dispatch prompt.
> Most agent implementations won't auto-load role files.

## Files

| File | What it contains | When to read |
|---|---|---|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Mermaid diagram, detailed steps, integration rules, final report format | Before first dispatch — complete execution manual |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | Resource constraints, behavioral constraints, failure handling, degradation modes | When encountering limits, handling failures, or needing degradation rules |
| [roles/*.md](roles/) | Each role's identity, success criteria, output format, inline persona | Before dispatching each teammate — extract Inline Persona |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | Required external skills and tools | **At startup** — validate dependencies, report missing, user decides |

## WHAT

虚拟形象设计要素：
- **骨架架构**: 骨骼层级、运动学系统、表情绑定、物理模拟
- **材质渲染**: 皮肤材质、服装贴图、光影效果、性能优化
- **造型设计**: 发型妆容、服装搭配、风格定位、个性表达

## WHEN

Use for:
- 设计 VRChat/VTuber 的高保真数字形象
- 虚拟偶像形象开发
- VR/AR 平台化身设计
- 直播虚拟主播形象
- 元宇宙数字身份

## NOT

Do NOT use for:
- 2D 漫画人物创作
- 静态角色插画
- 非实时渲染的角色设计
- 传统动画角色设计

## Inspiration

VRM 虚拟形象标准协议: https://vrm.dev/

核心原则：
1. 标准兼容 — 遵循 VRM 规范确保跨平台兼容
2. 性能优先 — 实时渲染性能与视觉质量平衡
3. 表情丰富 — 骨骼绑定支持多样化表情表达
4. 风格一致 — 造型、材质、骨架风格统一

## Usage

```markdown
请使用 virtual-avatar-stylist-team 设计虚拟形象：

[项目信息]
- 项目类型: [VRChat化身/VTuber主播/虚拟偶像]
- 目标平台: [VRChat/VRM兼容平台/直播平台]
- 性能要求: [高保真/中等/低配优化]

[可选详情]
- 身体类型: [写实/动漫/风格化]
- 风格参考: [参考图片或描述]
- 个性特征: [性格、气质、背景故事]
- 特殊需求: [特殊表情、物理效果、服装变化]
```

## Output Example

```markdown
## 虚拟形象设计报告

### 1. 骨架架构设计

**骨架结构概览**
| 参数 | 数值 | 说明 |
|------|------|------|
```

skills.name: ip-protection-team
skills.short_desc: 3角色并行IP保护团队，包含商标专家、著作权专家、商业秘密专家。可全周期审计企业知识产权资产，覆盖商标注册、著作权归属、商业秘密分类，生成综合保护策略报告。
skills.detail_desc:
# 知识产权全周期保护团队

本团队采用并行分解模式，由商标、著作权、商业秘密三个专业角色独立分析企业知识产权资产的保护状态。每个角色专注于其专业领域，最终整合形成全面的知识产权保护策略报告。

## Workflow

0. **Pre-flight: check dependencies** — read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and verify all items are available. Report results to the user:
   - **All passed** → proceed to step 1.
   - **Some missing** → list each missing item with its `purpose` field, distinguish `required: true` (will likely fail) vs `required: false` (degraded). **Ask the user whether to proceed or abort.**

1. **Leader: gather context** — collect enterprise IP asset inventory, existing registrations, licensing agreements, confidentiality policies, and target market information. Distribute identical context to all three roles.

2. **Parallel IP audit** — dispatch all three roles simultaneously:
   - **trademark-specialist**: analyzes trademark registration status and international protection strategy
   - **copyright-specialist**: evaluates copyright ownership and digital content protection
   - **trade-secret-specialist**: audits trade secret classification and access controls

3. **Leader: integrate findings** — collect all role outputs, compose by domain slice, identify cross-domain gaps.

4. **Final: emit IP Protection Strategy Report** — comprehensive report with domain-specific findings, cross-domain recommendations, and action priorities.

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| trademark-specialist | Analyzes trademark registration and international strategy | Every run, parallel with other roles | IP inventory, trademark registrations, target markets | None | [roles/trademark-specialist.md](roles/trademark-specialist.md) |
| copyright-specialist | Evaluates copyright ownership and digital protection | Every run, parallel with other roles | IP inventory, licensing agreements, digital content | None | [roles/copyright-specialist.md](roles/copyright-specialist.md) |
| trade-secret-specialist | Audits trade secret classification and controls | Every run, parallel with other roles | IP inventory, confidentiality policies, employee data | None | [roles/trade-secret-specialist.md](roles/trade-secret-specialist.md) |

> Before dispatching each teammate, read the corresponding role file and extract the `## Inline Persona for Teammate` section — paste it directly into the dispatch prompt. Most adopting agents do NOT auto-load role files for teammates.

## Files

| File | What it contains | When to read |
|---|---|---|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Mermaid diagram, step-by-step protocol, integration rules, Final Report format | Before first dispatch — the complete playbook |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | Resource limits, behavioral constraints, failure handling and degraded modes | When hitting limits, handling failures, or needing degraded-mode rules |
| [roles/*.md](roles/) | Per-role identity, success criteria, output schema, Inline Persona for Teammate | Before dispatching each teammate — extract Inline Persona |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | External skills and tools required to run | **Startup** — verify deps, report missing items, user decides go/no-go |

skills.name: k8s-cluster-optimization-team
skills.short_desc:
WHAT: 4-role K8s cluster optimization team (Resource Quota Analyst + HPA Strategist + Stability Guardian + Optimization Synthesizer) that surfaces efficiency-stability-scaling trade-offs instead of papering over them.
WHEN: Cloud-native applications experience frequent OOM events, resource waste, scaling bottlenecks, or cost alerts requiring multi-angle optimization analysis.
NOT: Basic container image building, single-workload debugging, or infrastructure provisioning.
skills.detail_desc:
# K8s Cluster Optimization Team

A 4-role parallel adversarial team (A+B pattern) that solves the single-agent failure mode where efficiency-focused analysis ignores stability risks, and stability-focused analysis ignores cost opportunities. Three specialist analysts run in parallel with isolated visibility, each examining a disjoint slice of the optimization problem space. The Optimization Synthesizer then integrates their findings with adversarial cross-check, surfacing contradictions rather than silently resolving them.

## Workflow

0. **Pre-flight: check dependencies** — read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and verify.
   Report missing items: `required: true` = likely fails without it; `required: false` = degraded but functional. **User decides** whether to proceed.
   The team can run on inline-persona-only mode if all skills are missing (current state: no K8s-specific skills found, all tools optional).
   Note: dependencies were populated during authoring via Stage 2 auto-matching (local scan). Community enrichment recommended for K8s domain skills.

1. **Context extraction and dispatch preparation** — Leader extracts relevant data slices from user-provided cluster state (ResourceQuota objects, HPA/VPA/CA configs, Pod metrics, OOMKill events, restart counts) and problem context (OOM events, cost alerts, performance issues). Prepares three dispatch prompts with placeholders filled. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 1 for details.

2. **Parallel analyst execution** — resource-quota-analyst, hpa-strategist, stability-guardian run simultaneously with isolated visibility (A-pattern isolation). Each analyst independently examines their assigned slice and produces findings matching their Output Schema. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 2 and [bind.md](bind.md) § Resource Constraints for limits.

3. **Adversarial synthesis** — optimization-synthesizer receives outputs from all three analysts, compares recommendations, identifies contradictions and tensions (efficiency vs stability, scaling vs quota, cost vs reliability), produces prioritized optimization roadmap with trade-off rationale. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 3 for details.

4. **Final: emit K8s Cluster Optimization Report** — Leader integrates synthesis report into final deliverable, surfaces contradictions verbatim (does NOT mediate), presents prioritized roadmap, highlights unresolved trade-offs requiring user decision. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) § Final Report Format for structure.

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| resource-quota-analyst | Analyze ResourceQuota and Pod resource usage for inefficiencies and cost optimization | Every run, parallel with hpa-strategist and stability-guardian | Cluster state (ResourceQuota, Pod requests/limits, namespace utilization) + problem context | k8s-cost (community) | [roles/resource-quota-analyst.md](roles/resource-quota-analyst.md) |
| hpa-strategist | Analyze HPA/VPA/CA configurations for scaling readiness and capacity headroom | Every run, parallel with resource-quota-analyst and stability-guardian | Cluster state (HPA/VPA/CA configs, workload metrics, scaling history) + problem context | k8s-autoscaling (community) | [roles/hpa-strategist.md](roles/hpa-strategist.md) |
| stability-guardian | Analyze OOMKill events, Pod restarts, and resource contention for stability risks | Every run, parallel with resource-quota-analyst and hpa-strategist | Cluster state (Pod status, OOMKill events, restart counts, node pressure) + problem context + other analyst recommendations (for adversarial cross-check) | kubernetes-ops (community) | [roles/stability-guardian.md](roles/stability-guardian.md) |
| optimization-synthesizer | Integrate findings, surface contradictions, produce prioritized optimization roadmap | After all analysts complete (serial) | Outputs from all three analysts + problem context | None (inline persona only) | [roles/optimization-synthesizer.md](roles/optimization-synthesizer.md) |

> Before dispatching each teammate, read the corresponding role file and extract the
> `## Inline Persona for Teammate` section — paste it directly into the dispatch prompt.
> Most adopting agents do NOT auto-load role files for teammates.

## Files

| File | What it contains | When to read |

| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Mermaid diagram, step-by-step protocol, integration rules, Final Report format | Before first dispatch — the complete playbook |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | Resource limits, behavioral constraints, failure handling and degraded modes | When hitting limits, handling failures, or needing degraded-mode rules |
| [roles/*.md](roles/) | Per-role identity, success criteria, output schema, Inline Persona for Teammate | Before dispatching each teammate — extract Inline Persona |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | External skills and tools required to run | **Startup** — verify deps, report missing items, user decides go/no-go |

skills.detail_desc:
# motion-graphics-director-team

Pacing Strategist + Visual Stylist + Transition Critic for animated typography and graphics. A three-expert team dedicated to producing high-end explainer videos, title sequences, and motion graphics that combine precise timing with stunning visual aesthetics.

## Workflow

0. **Pre-check: Validate Dependencies** — Read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and validate.
   Report missing items: `required: true` = may fail without this; `required: false` = degraded but functional. **User decides** whether to continue.
   This team has no external dependencies — all roles operate on inline role knowledge.

1. **Parallel Review Stage** — Leader dispatches Pacing Strategist, Visual Stylist, and Transition Critic simultaneously. Each role works independently. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Stage 1.

2. **Leader Integration Stage** — Leader integrates all three outputs, cross-validates pacing-visual-transition alignment, marks agreements and conflicts. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Stage 2.

3. **Final: Output Motion Graphics Design Report** — Leader outputs comprehensive motion graphics design report with pacing structure, visual style, and transition evaluation. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) § Final Report Format.

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| pacing-strategist | Define timing rhythm | Stage 1, parallel | `{CONTENT_DURATION}`, `{EMOTIONAL_ARC}`, `{BEAT_STRUCTURE}` | none | [roles/pacing-strategist.md](roles/pacing-strategist.md) |
| visual-stylist | Design visual style | Stage 1, parallel | `{BRAND_IDENTITY}`, `{TYPOGRAPHY_REQ}`, `{AESTHETIC_DIRECTION}` | none | [roles/visual-stylist.md](roles/visual-stylist.md) |
| transition-critic | Evaluate transitions | Stage 1, parallel | `{TRANSITION_TYPES}`, `{FLOW_CONTINUITY}`, `{VISUAL_COHERENCE}` | none | [roles/transition-critic.md](roles/transition-critic.md) |

> Before dispatching each teammate, read the corresponding role file and extract
> the `## Inline Persona for Teammate` section — paste it directly into the dispatch prompt.
> Most agent implementations won't auto-load role files.

## Files

| File | What it contains | When to read |
|---|---|---|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Mermaid diagram, detailed steps, integration rules, final report format | Before first dispatch — complete execution manual |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | Resource constraints, behavioral constraints, failure handling, degradation modes | When encountering limits, handling failures, or needing degradation rules |
| [roles/*.md](roles/) | Each role's identity, success criteria, output format, inline persona | Before dispatching each teammate — extract Inline Persona |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | Required external skills and tools | **At startup** — validate dependencies, report missing, user decides |

## WHAT

Motion graphics design elements:
- **Pacing Structure**: Timing rhythm, beat structure, emotional pacing curve
- **Visual Style**: Typography treatment, aesthetic direction, brand alignment
- **Transition Design**: Flow continuity, visual coherence, transition quality

## WHEN

Use for:
- High-end explainer videos and educational content
- Title sequences and opening credits
- Brand motion identity and animated logos
- Social media motion content
- Presentation and corporate motion graphics

## NOT

Do NOT use for:
- Single-frame art or static design
- Non-animated typography
- Video editing without motion design focus
- 3D animation (different workflow requirements)

## Inspiration

Motionographer - Motion Design Trends: https://motionographer.com/

Core principles:
1. Pacing is emotion — Timing rhythm creates emotional response
2. Style serves story — Visual aesthetics support narrative purpose
3. Transitions are invisible — Great transitions feel natural, not forced
4. Brand consistency — Motion style aligns with brand identity

## Usage

```markdown
Please use motion-graphics-director-team to design:

[Project Information]
- Project type: [Explainer/Title Sequence/Brand Motion/Social]
- Duration: [Total length in seconds]
- Brand identity: [Brand personality, visual style]

[Optional Details]
- Emotional arc: [Build-up/Peak/Release pattern]
- Typography requirements: [Font constraints, readability needs]
- Platform: [Web/Social/TV/Film]
- Reference style: [Minimal/Dynamic/Elegant/Playful]
```

## Output Example

```markdown
## Motion Graphics Design Report

### 1. Pacing Structure Design

**Timing Framework**
| Parameter | Specification | Notes |
|-----------|---------------|-------|
| Total duration | 60 seconds | Standard explainer length |
| Beat count | 24 beats | 4 beats per 10-second segment |
| Primary rhythm | 120 BPM | Moderate energy pace |
| Pacing variation | 3 levels | Slow intro, fast middle, calm close |

**Beat Structure Map**
| Segment | Duration | Beat Count | Pacing Type | Emotional State |
|---------|----------|------------|-------------|-----------------|
| Intro | 0-10s | 4 beats | Slow build | Curiosity, anticipation |
```
