| Close | 50-60s | 4 beats | Calm resolve | Satisfaction, CTA |

**Emotional Pacing Curve**
| Curve Segment | Shape | Timing | Visual Density | Audio Sync |
|---------------|-------|--------|----------------|------------|
| Opening rise | Exponential | 0-15s | Increasing | Music intro build |
| Peak plateau | Flat high | 15-45s | Maximum | Full music energy |
| Closing descent | Linear | 45-60s | Decreasing | Music fade out |

**Key Moment Timing**
| Moment | Timestamp | Duration | Pacing Role | Visual Treatment |
|--------|-----------|----------|-------------|------------------|
| Title reveal | 3s | 2s | First hook | Bold typography animation |
| Problem intro | 12s | 3s | Engagement | Dynamic text + icon |
| Solution reveal | 25s | 4s | Peak moment | Hero animation |
| Benefit showcase | 35s | 5s | Reinforcement | Sequential reveals |
| CTA appearance | 55s | 3s | Final hook | Clear, actionable |

### 2. Visual Style Design

**Typography Treatment**
| Element | Font Family | Weight | Size Range | Animation Style |
|---------|-------------|--------|------------|-----------------|
| Headlines | Brand Sans | Bold | 48-72pt | Scale + fade |
| Body text | Brand Sans | Regular | 24-36pt | Slide + reveal |
| Emphasis | Brand Sans | Black | Same as parent | Color pop + pulse |
| Numbers | Brand Mono | Medium | 60-96pt | Count-up animation |
| CTA | Brand Sans | Bold | 36-48pt | Bounce + glow |

**Color Motion Palette**
| Color Role | Hex Value | Motion Usage | Transition Behavior |
|------------|-----------|--------------|---------------------|
| Primary | #FF6B35 | Key elements, headlines | Pulse on emphasis |
| Secondary | #004E89 | Supporting text, icons | Fade transitions |
| Accent | #F7C59F | Highlights, CTAs | Glow effect |
| Background | #1A1A2E | Base layer | Gradient shift |
| Neutral | #EFEFEF | Body text | Static or subtle fade |

**Aesthetic Direction**
| Style Dimension | Direction | Implementation | Brand Alignment |
|-----------------|-----------|----------------|-----------------|
| Overall feel | Modern dynamic | Clean lines, bold moves | Matches brand energy |
| Complexity | Medium | 2-3 layers per scene | Readable yet engaging |
| Motion type | Geometric | Shapes, lines, transforms | Tech-forward brand |
| Texture | Minimal | Flat design, subtle gradients | Clean brand identity |
| Depth | 2.5D | Layered parallax | Modern without 3D cost |

**Visual Hierarchy Motion**
| Hierarchy Level | Motion Treatment | Timing Priority | Visual Weight |
|-----------------|------------------|-----------------|---------------|
| Level 1 (Hero) | Full animation, emphasis | First in sequence | Maximum |
| Level 2 (Key) | Partial animation | Second in sequence | High |
| Level 3 (Support) | Subtle motion | Parallel or follow | Medium |
| Level 4 (Background) | Ambient motion | Continuous | Low |

### 3. Transition Evaluation

**Transition Types Inventory**
| Transition ID | Type | Duration | Use Case | Quality Score |
|---------------|------|----------|----------|---------------|
| T01 | Fade | 0.5s | Scene endings | 85/100 |
| T02 | Slide | 0.3s | Sequential reveals | 90/100 |
| T03 | Scale | 0.4s | Emphasis moments | 88/100 |
| T04 | Morph | 0.6s | Shape transformations | 82/100 |
| T05 | Mask | 0.5s | Text reveals | 87/100 |
| T06 | Wipe | 0.3s | Section changes | 85/100 |

**Flow Continuity Assessment**
| Flow Segment | Transition Chain | Continuity Score | Issues Identified |
|--------------|------------------|------------------|-------------------|
| Intro → Hook | Fade → Scale | 92/100 | Smooth, natural |
| Hook → Core | Slide → Morph | 88/100 | Slightly abrupt |
| Core → Reinforce | Scale → Fade | 90/100 | Good energy shift |
| Reinforce → Close | Fade → Wipe | 85/100 | Could be smoother |

**Visual Coherence Check**
| Coherence Aspect | Score | Notes | Recommendations |
|------------------|-------|-------|-----------------|
| Color consistency | 95/100 | All transitions use palette colors | Maintain |
| Typography flow | 90/100 | Font sizes transition smoothly | Minor adjustment |
| Motion language | 88/100 | Some transitions differ in style | Standardize |
| Pacing alignment | 92/100 | Transitions match beat structure | Excellent |
| Brand alignment | 94/100 | Motion style matches brand | Maintain |

**Transition Quality Criteria**
| Criterion | Weight | Assessment Method | Threshold |
|-----------|--------|-------------------|-----------|
| Smoothness | 30% | Frame-by-frame review | No jarring cuts |
| Purpose | 25% | Narrative function check | Every transition serves story |
| Timing | 20% | Beat alignment check | Sync with music beats |
| Style match | 15% | Visual consistency check | Matches overall aesthetic |
| Performance | 10% | Render test | No frame drops |

### 4. Cross-Dimension Validation

- ✅ Pacing structure supports visual hierarchy (hero elements at peak moments)
- ✅ Visual style enables smooth transitions (consistent motion language)
- ✅ Transitions respect pacing rhythm (timing matches beat structure)
- ⚠ Some transitions could be standardized for better coherence

### 5. Production Recommendations

| Priority | Item | Output | Timeline |

| P0 | Pacing timeline | Beat map + keyframes | Day 1-2 |

| P4 | Refinement | Final polish | Day 7-10 |

skills.name: worldbuilding-team
skills.short_desc:
3-role parallel worldbuilding team (Lore + Geography + Magic Architects) for consistent fictional worlds.
Use when planning novels, RPGs, or cinematic universes requiring coherent world architecture.
Do NOT use for standalone creative exercises or narrative content generation.
skills.detail_desc:
# Worldbuilding Team

A 3-role parallel decomposition team that architects consistent fictional worlds across three dimensions: Lore (history, culture, mythology), Geography (terrain, climate, biomes), and Magic (supernatural systems). Each architect works independently, then the Leader integrates and surfaces cross-domain coordination conflicts for user resolution. This solves the single-agent failure mode where one creator misses internal consistency across dimensions.

## Workflow

0. **Pre-flight: check dependencies** — read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and verify. Report missing items: `required: true` = likely fails without it; `required: false` = degraded but functional. **User decides** whether to proceed. This team has no external dependencies — all architects operate on native creative reasoning.

1. **Context distribution** — Leader parses world premise, extracts genre/tone/complexity constraints, prepares dispatch prompts for each architect with `{WORLD_PREMISE_PLACEHOLDER}` and `{SCOPE_GUIDANCE_PLACEHOLDER}` filled. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 1 for details.

2. **Parallel architecture** — lore-architect, geography-architect, magic-architect run simultaneously, each producing their domain output following their Output Schema. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 2 for parallel execution details.

3. **Cross-domain consistency check** — Leader extracts all coordination flags from each architect's output, identifies conflicts and gaps, produces Consistency Check Report. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 3 for check logic.

4. **Conflict resolution (if needed)** — If conflicts exist, Leader surfaces them verbatim to user for resolution decision. Leader never auto-resolves. See [bind.md](bind.md) Behavioral Constraints for conflict handling rules.

5. **Final: emit World Bible** — Leader integrates all domain outputs into unified World Bible document with cross-domain connection notes. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Final Report Format for output structure.

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| lore-architect | Architect historical, cultural, mythological foundations with causal chains | every run, parallel with geography and magic | world_premise, scope_guidance | none | [roles/lore-architect.md](roles/lore-architect.md) |
| geography-architect | Architect physical terrain, climate, biomes with geological plausibility | every run, parallel with lore and magic | world_premise, scope_guidance | none | [roles/geography-architect.md](roles/geography-architect.md) |
| magic-architect | Architect supernatural systems with rules, costs, limits | every run, parallel with lore and geography | world_premise, scope_guidance | none | [roles/magic-architect.md](roles/magic-architect.md) |

> Before dispatching each teammate, read the corresponding role file and extract the `## Inline Persona for Teammate` section — paste it directly into the dispatch prompt. Most adopting agents do NOT auto-load role files for teammates.

## Files

| File | What it contains | When to read |
|---|---|---|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Mermaid diagram, step-by-step protocol, integration rules, Final Report format | Before first dispatch — the complete playbook |

skills.name: data-visualization-storyteller-team
skills.display_name: 数据可视化叙事团队技能
skills.short_desc:
A 4-role pipeline for presentation-grade data stories: audience framing, chart design, narrative critique, and delivery packaging.
Use when complex analytics must land with non-technical stakeholders through honest visuals.
Not for default spreadsheet charts or decks without a discernible story arc.
skills.detail_desc:
# Data visualization storyteller team

Charts plus captions plus sequencing separate insight from decoration. This pipeline prevents premature viz polish before the editorial stance is clear.

## Workflow

This Teamskill is a strict **C-pattern (serial gated pipeline)** with 4 fixed roles. Each stage is dispatched after the prior output is available, so story framing is frozen before chart encoding, and integrity critique happens before packaging.

0. **Pre-flight: check dependencies** — read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and note optional-skill gaps and impact.

1. **Leader extracts story constraints** — audience literacy, decision stakes, medium (slides/memo/dashboard), ethics constraints, and what raw metrics are available. Leader does not “pick charts” yet.

2. **audience-story-architect defines the storyline** — lock the one-sentence takeaway + ordered story beats + comparison baselines, and identify any beats that are unsupported by data.

3. **viz-design-lead specifies encodings** — map beats to chart grammar, scales, accessibility posture, and annotation strategy. Prefer clarity over novelty.

4. **chart-integrity-critic audits distortions** — flag truncation, dual-axis misuse, cherry windows, binning artifacts, and lie-factor risks with severity and remediation/waiver rationale.

5. **viz-story-package-lead assembles the delivery package** — produce a run-of-show table (beat → visual → caption → speaker note), plus residual-risk callouts for any accepted waivers.

6. **Leader emits final report** — the **Data story package** with an executive summary, visual spec table, integrity resolutions, and an appendix-ready backlog.

## Roles

| id | Focus | Stage | File |
|---|---|---|---|
| audience-story-architect | Storyframe | 1 | [roles/audience-story-architect.md](roles/audience-story-architect.md) |
| viz-design-lead | Encoding | 2 | [roles/viz-design-lead.md](roles/viz-design-lead.md) |
| chart-integrity-critic | Integrity | 3 | [roles/chart-integrity-critic.md](roles/chart-integrity-critic.md) |
| viz-story-package-lead | Package | 4 | [roles/viz-story-package-lead.md](roles/viz-story-package-lead.md) |

> Paste each role’s `## Inline Persona for Teammate` block into teammate prompts.

## Files

| File | Contents |
|---|---|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Flow + gates |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | Limits |
| [roles/*.md](roles/) | Personas |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | Deps |

skills.name: web-security-hardening-team
skills.short_desc:
WHAT: 6-role web security hardening team (4 parallel specialists + coordinator + validator) that analyzes HTTP headers, CSP, cookies/CORS, and TLS configuration against Mozilla Web Security Guidelines.
WHEN: Application pre-launch security hardening — final configuration review before deployment.
Do NOT use for application logic vulnerability audit (SQL injection, auth flaws) — this team focuses on server/configuration-level security headers and policies only.
skills.detail_desc:
# Web Security Hardening Team

A 6-role team following **B + C pattern** (parallel decomposition → pipeline finalization) for comprehensive web application security hardening. Four parallel specialists analyze disjoint security domains (HTTP headers, CSP, cookies/CORS, TLS), then a coordinator synthesizes findings into a unified plan, and a validator verifies configuration correctness before delivery.

**Failure mode solved**: Single-agent security reviews often miss domain-specific vulnerabilities due to cognitive blind spots — one reviewer focusing on XSS may overlook cookie security or TLS downgrade risks. This team ensures complete coverage across all web security configuration domains.

## Workflow

0. **Pre-flight: check dependencies** — read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and verify curl/openssl availability (optional tools for live URL probing). Report missing items. **User decides** whether to proceed. The team can run on configuration file analysis alone if tools are missing.

1. **Stage 1: Parallel Security Domain Analysis** — 4 specialists run simultaneously, each analyzing their assigned domain:
   - [http-header-specialist](roles/http-header-specialist.md): HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy
   - [csp-policy-specialist](roles/csp-policy-specialist.md): CSP directives, XSS vectors, unsafe-inline/unsafe-eval
   - [cookie-cors-specialist](roles/cookie-cors-specialist.md): Cookie attributes, CORS headers, CSRF risks
   - [tls-resource-specialist](roles/tls-resource-specialist.md): HTTPS, TLS config, SRI for external resources
   
   See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 1 for gate criteria (all 4 must return valid output).

2. **Stage 2: Findings Synthesis** — [security-hardening-coordinator](roles/security-hardening-coordinator.md) receives all Stage 1 outputs, consolidates findings, prioritizes by severity (IMMEDIATE/SHORT-TERM/LONG-TERM), identifies dependencies and conflicts. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 2 for gate criteria.

3. **Stage 3: Configuration Validation** — [config-validator](roles/config-validator.md) validates proposed configurations for syntax, semantic safety, browser compatibility, and provides nginx/Apache implementation examples. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 3 for gate criteria.

4. **Final: emit Web Security Hardening Report** — Leader integrates all outputs into final report with priority tiers, implementation dependencies, conflicts, and server examples. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) for Final Report format.

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| http-header-specialist | Analyze HTTP security headers | Stage 1 (parallel with 3 others) | target_info, existing_headers | none | [roles/http-header-specialist.md](roles/http-header-specialist.md) |
| csp-policy-specialist | Evaluate CSP for XSS protection | Stage 1 (parallel with 3 others) | target_info, existing_csp | none | [roles/csp-policy-specialist.md](roles/csp-policy-specialist.md) |
| cookie-cors-specialist | Audit cookies and CORS | Stage 1 (parallel with 3 others) | target_info, cookies_observed, cors_headers | none | [roles/cookie-cors-specialist.md](roles/cookie-cors-specialist.md) |
| tls-resource-specialist | Verify TLS and SRI | Stage 1 (parallel with 3 others) | target_info, tls_info, external_resources | openssl (optional) | [roles/tls-resource-specialist.md](roles/tls-resource-specialist.md) |
| security-hardening-coordinator | Synthesize findings into plan | Stage 2 (after Stage 1 gate) | all 4 Stage 1 outputs | none | [roles/security-hardening-coordinator.md](roles/security-hardening-coordinator.md) |
| config-validator | Validate configurations | Stage 3 (after Stage 2 gate) | hardening_plan, target_server_type | none | [roles/config-validator.md](roles/config-validator.md) |

skills.name: real-estate-team
skills.short_desc:
5-role real-estate Teamskill (Researcher → Valuator → [Listing Writer ∥ Lead Nurture] → Memory/CRM) producing a sourced property brief, a defensible AVM range, a listing package, and a qualified-lead record.
Use when an agent must research, valuate, write listing copy, and handle inbound leads in one orchestrated run with state persisted across re-runs.
Do NOT use for single-role tasks or for jurisdictions where an AVM cannot be defended with comparables.
skills.detail_desc:
# Real Estate Team

A Mixed C+B Teamskill that orchestrates the full real-estate workflow from data gathering to lead handling. The Orchestrator (Leader) drives the run; five specialized teammates handle research, valuation, listing copy, lead nurture, and stateful CRM persistence. The pattern solves the failure mode where a single-agent skill collapses research rigour, valuation discipline, and copywriting tone into a blurred mid-quality output, and where re-runs on small triggers (e.g. a price drop) repeat upstream work that was already done.

The "Orchestrator" agent named in the user's spec is the Leader of this Teamskill — there is no separate `orchestrator.md` role file because the Leader is implied by the spec (it dispatches teammates and integrates outputs). The five role files are the specialized teammates.

## Workflow

0. **Pre-flight: check dependencies** — read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and verify `python`, `curl`, `sqlite3` are available. Report missing items: `required: true` will block the affected role; the team can run on inline-persona-only mode for non-tool-dependent roles. **User decides** go/no-go.
1. **Load prior context** — the Leader dispatches `memory-crm` in `mode: "load"` to retrieve any prior property brief, valuation history, listing drafts, and lead interactions for this `property_id`. Staleness is flagged per role's thresholds in [bind.md](bind.md) § Resource Constraints.
2. **Research (or skip)** — if loaded context contains a fresh property brief (`stale: false`), the Leader skips Step 2 to save work. Otherwise the Leader dispatches `property-researcher` to assemble a new sourced JSON brief. Quality gate enforced before proceeding — see [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 2.
3. **Valuation** — the Leader dispatches `valuator` with the brief; output is a defensible price range with confidence score and off-market flag. If the brief flagged `comps_insufficient`, the Valuator runs in degraded indicative-only mode.
4. **Parallel output stage** — the Leader merges property + valuation into one brief and dispatches `listing-writer` and `lead-nurture` in parallel (B-pattern fan-out, `max_parallel_teammates: 2`).
5. **Persist** — the Leader dispatches `memory-crm` in `mode: "persist"` to version every artifact (property brief, valuation, listing package, lead state). Idempotency detection prevents version churn on no-op re-runs.
6. **Final: emit Real Estate Run Report** — Leader integrates all role outputs into the report defined in [workflow.md](workflow.md) § Final Report Format. Contradictions across roles are surfaced verbatim, never mediated. `[ROLE MISSING]` markers are preserved verbatim — the Leader does not paper over failures.

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| property-researcher | Sourced JSON property brief | Step 2 (skipped if prior brief is fresh) | property_id, address, property_type, optional prior_brief | curl, python | [roles/property-researcher.md](roles/property-researcher.md) |
| valuator | Weighted-comp AVM range + confidence | Step 3 (always after Researcher) | property_brief, optional asking_price, optional condition_signal | python | [roles/valuator.md](roles/valuator.md) |
| listing-writer | Headline, description, 3 captions, SEO meta | Step 5a (parallel with lead-nurture) | merged property+valuation brief, language, optional desired_tone | (none) | [roles/listing-writer.md](roles/listing-writer.md) |
| lead-nurture | Lead state + verdict + drip schedule | Step 5b (parallel with listing-writer) | merged_brief.valuation, lead_id, inbound_message, viewing_calendar | python, sqlite3 | [roles/lead-nurture.md](roles/lead-nurture.md) |
| memory-crm | Load prior context (entry) / persistence receipt (exit) | Step 1 + Step 5 (always twice) | mode, property_id, store_uri, artifacts_to_persist | sqlite3, python | [roles/memory-crm.md](roles/memory-crm.md) |

> Before dispatching each teammate, read the corresponding role file and extract the
> `## Inline Persona for Teammate` section — paste it directly into the dispatch prompt.
> Most adopting agents do NOT auto-load role files for teammates.

## Files

| File | What it contains | When to read |
|---|---|---|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Mermaid diagram (Mixed C+B), 7-step protocol with quality gates, integration rules, Final Report format | Before first dispatch — the complete playbook |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | Resource limits, behavioral constraints, failure handling, degraded modes (zero comps, missing Chinese language support, lead overflow) | When hitting limits, handling failures, or needing degraded-mode rules |
| [roles/\*.md](roles/) | Per-role identity, success criteria, output schema, Inline Persona for Teammate | Before dispatching each teammate — extract Inline Persona |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | python, curl, sqlite3 (skills: [] — community enrichment recommended for real-estate-specific skills) | **Startup** — verify deps, report missing items, user decides go/no-go |

skills.detail_desc:
# ui-ux-prototyping-team

UI/UX design review team for validating high-fidelity designs before development handoff. Three specialists collaborate: Usability Critic evaluates against heuristics, Visual Consistency Auditor ensures design system compliance, Interaction Designer validates interaction patterns.

## Workflow

0. **Pre-check: Validate Dependencies** — Read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and validate.
   Report missing items: `required: true` = may fail without this; `required: false` = degraded but functional. **User decides** whether to continue.
   This team has no external dependencies — all roles operate on inline role knowledge.

1. **Parallel Review Stage** — Leader dispatches Usability Critic, Visual Consistency Auditor, and Interaction Designer simultaneously. Each role works independently. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Stage 1.

2. **Leader Integration Stage** — Leader integrates all three outputs, cross-validates alignment, marks agreements and conflicts. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Stage 2.

3. **Final: Output Design Review Report** — Leader outputs comprehensive design review with usability findings, consistency issues, and interaction recommendations. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) § Final Report Format.

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| usability-critic | Evaluate usability heuristics | Stage 1, parallel | `{SCREENS}`, `{USER_FLOWS}`, `{PERSONAS}` | none | [roles/usability-critic.md](roles/usability-critic.md) |
| visual-consistency-auditor | Audit visual consistency | Stage 1, parallel | `{SCREENS}`, `{DESIGN_SYSTEM}`, `{BRAND_GUIDELINES}` | none | [roles/visual-consistency-auditor.md](roles/visual-consistency-auditor.md) |
| interaction-designer | Review interaction patterns | Stage 1, parallel | `{SCREENS}`, `{INTERACTION_PATTERNS}`, `{ANIMATION_SPECS}` | none | [roles/interaction-designer.md](roles/interaction-designer.md) |

> Before dispatching each teammate, read the corresponding role file and extract
> the `## Inline Persona for Teammate` section — paste it directly into the dispatch prompt.
> Most agent implementations won't auto-load role files.

## Files

| File | What it contains | When to read |
|---|---|---|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Mermaid diagram, detailed steps, integration rules, final report format | Before first dispatch — complete execution manual |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | Resource constraints, behavioral constraints, failure handling, degradation modes | When encountering limits, handling failures, or needing degradation rules |
| [roles/*.md](roles/) | Each role's identity, success criteria, output format, inline persona | Before dispatching each teammate — extract Inline Persona |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | Required external skills and tools | **At startup** — validate dependencies, report missing, user decides |

## WHAT

Validates high-fidelity UI/UX designs through:
- **Usability Review**: Nielsen's 10 heuristics evaluation, pain point identification, learnability assessment
- **Visual Consistency**: Design system compliance, cross-screen consistency, accessibility standards
- **Interaction Quality**: Pattern validation, micro-interaction review, feedback and affordance clarity

## WHEN

Use for:
- High-fidelity mockups before development handoff
- Prototype validation before user testing
- Design review checkpoints in agile sprints
- Pre-release design quality assurance

## NOT

Do NOT use for:
- Paper sketches or low-fidelity wireframes
- Initial concept brainstorming
- Information architecture planning
- Content strategy development

## Inspiration

Nielsen's 10 Usability Heuristics: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/ten-usability-heuristics/

Core principles:
1. Visibility of system status
2. Match between system and real world
3. User control and freedom
4. Consistency and standards
5. Error prevention
6. Recognition rather than recall
7. Flexibility and efficiency of use
8. Aesthetic and minimalist design
9. Help users recognize, diagnose, and recover from errors
10. Help and documentation

## Usage

```markdown
Please use ui-ux-prototyping-team to review the following design:

[Design Information]
- Screens: [List of screens to review]
- User flows: [Key user journeys]
- Target users: [Primary personas]

[Optional Details]
- Design system: [Link to design system]
- Brand guidelines: [Link to brand guidelines]
- Known issues: [Any known concerns]
- Platform: [Web/Mobile/Desktop]
```

## Output Example

```markdown
## Design Review Report

### 1. Usability Findings

**Heuristic Evaluation Summary**
| Heuristic | Score | Issues Found | Priority |
|-----------|-------|--------------|----------|
| Visibility of system status | 8/10 | 2 minor | Low |
| User control and freedom | 6/10 | 3 moderate | Medium |
| Consistency and standards | 7/10 | 2 moderate | Medium |
| Error prevention | 5/10 | 4 issues | High |

**Critical Pain Points**
1. **[Issue]** Back button missing in checkout flow
   - Impact: Users feel trapped, potential abandonment
   - Recommendation: Add visible back navigation

2. **[Issue]** Form validation only on submit
   - Impact: Users frustrated by late error discovery
   - Recommendation: Implement real-time validation

### 2. Visual Consistency Issues

**Design System Compliance**
| Component | Compliance | Deviations |
|-----------|------------|-------------|
| Buttons | 90% | 2 screens use non-standard hover |
| Typography | 85% | Inconsistent line heights |
| Spacing | 75% | Multiple padding values used |

**Cross-Screen Consistency**
- ✅ Color palette consistent across all screens
- ⚠ Button styles vary between flows
- ❌ Icon sizes inconsistent (16px vs 20px)

**Accessibility Check**
- Color contrast: 3 issues found
- Focus states: Missing on 4 interactive elements
- Screen reader: Alt text missing on 12 images

### 3. Interaction Recommendations

**Pattern Validation**
| Pattern | Status | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|
| Navigation | ✅ Valid | Follows platform conventions |
| Forms | ⚠ Needs work | Missing inline validation |
| Modals | ❌ Issue | No escape key support |

**Micro-interaction Review**
- Button feedback: Good hover/active states
- Loading states: Missing on 3 async operations
- Success/error feedback: Inconsistent positioning

**Affordance Clarity**
- Interactive elements: 2 buttons lack visual affordance
- Disabled states: Unclear visual distinction
- Gesture hints: Missing for swipe actions

### 4. Cross-Validation

- ✅ Usability issues align with visual inconsistencies
- ✅ Interaction patterns support usability heuristics
- ⚠ Error prevention needs both visual and interaction fixes

### 5. Priority Matrix

| Priority | Count | Examples |
|----------|-------|----------|
| 🔴 Critical | 3 | Missing back navigation, no escape key, contrast failures |
| 🟡 Moderate | 7 | Form validation, spacing inconsistencies, missing focus states |
| 🟢 Minor | 12 | Icon size variations, hover state deviations |

### 6. Action Items

- [ ] Add back navigation to checkout flow
- [ ] Implement real-time form validation
- [ ] Fix color contrast issues (3 instances)
- [ ] Standardize button hover states
- [ ] Add focus states to interactive elements
- [ ] Implement escape key for modals
- [ ] Add loading states for async operations
```

# 市场预测团队

该团队以“职责分离 + 质量门控”的方式协同完成复杂研究任务，避免单角色在关键判断上同时当运动员和裁判。每个角色只对本阶段产出负责，由 Leader 最终整合为可执行决策包。

## Workflow

0. **前置检查：验证依赖** — 读取 [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml)，确认依赖可用并标注缺失影响。
1. **driver-modeler**：定义核心驱动因子与可量化假设。
2. **forecast-builder**：建立基线预测并执行回测检验。
3. **scenario-challenger**：构造反事实情景挑战主预测。
4. **decision-synthesizer**：整合预测分歧并给出决策建议。
5. **Leader 组装最终交付** — 输出 **市场预测决策包**，包含结论、证据、风险与行动项。

## Roles

| id | 职责 | 何时派发 | 输入 | 关键依赖 | 角色文件 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| driver-modeler | 定义核心驱动因子与可量化假设。 | 阶段 1 | 任务简报 | forecast, python | [roles/driver-modeler.md](roles/driver-modeler.md) |
| forecast-builder | 建立基线预测并执行回测检验。 | 阶段 2 | 阶段 1 输出 | forecast, python | [roles/forecast-builder.md](roles/forecast-builder.md) |
| scenario-challenger | 构造反事实情景挑战主预测。 | 阶段 3 | 阶段 2 输出 | forecast, python | [roles/scenario-challenger.md](roles/scenario-challenger.md) |
| decision-synthesizer | 整合预测分歧并给出决策建议。 | 阶段 4 | 阶段 3 输出 | forecast, python | [roles/decision-synthesizer.md](roles/decision-synthesizer.md) |

> 派发每位队友前，请先读取对应角色文件中的 `## Inline Persona for Teammate` 段落，
> 并将其原样粘贴到派发提示词中。
> 大多数接入 Agent 不会自动加载角色文件。

## Files

| 文件 | 内容 | 何时读取 |
|---|---|---|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Mermaid 图、完整步骤、门控规则、最终报告模板 | 首次派发前（完整作战手册） |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | 资源限制、行为约束、失败处理与降级机制 | 遇到限制、失败处理或降级执行时 |
| [roles/*.md](roles/) | 各角色身份、成功标准、边界、输出结构、Inline Persona | 派发每个队友前（提取 Inline Persona） |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | 运行所需技能与工具 | **启动时**（核对依赖并报告缺失，由用户决定是否继续） |

一个四阶段专业化流水线团队，专注于亚马逊品牌化跨境经营。每个角色负责一个阶段：A+页面、广告、合规、售后。此模式解决了单一角色模糊阶段边界、导致品牌出海策略不系统的问题。

skills.name: static-analysis-rule-builder-team
skills.display_name: 静态分析规则构建团队
skills.short_desc:
WHAT: Expert team for building custom static analysis (AST-based) and linting rules.
WHEN: Standardizing code quality across large engineering organizations.
NOT: For manual per-PR reviews.
skills.detail_desc:
# Static Analysis Rule Builder Team

Expert team for designing custom AST-based static analysis and linting rules, following Google Tricorder principles: high-signal (low false positives), actionable (clear remediation), and workflow-integrated (gradual rollout, not blocking gates).

Inspired by [Google Open Source Static Analysis Guidelines](https://opensource.google/documentation/reference/static-analysis).

## Workflow

This team follows a **B + C mixed pattern**:

1. **Stage 1 (Parallel)**: Security, Performance, and Readability experts design rules independently on disjoint slices
2. **Stage 2 (Pipeline)**: Rule Architect integrates outputs, resolves overlaps, produces configuration scaffold
3. **Stage 3 (Pipeline)**: Test Validator designs test cases, verifies AST patterns, flags FP/FN risks
4. **Stage 4 (Pipeline)**: Documentation Writer produces reference docs, how-to guides, adoption guide

See [workflow.md](workflow.md) for detailed steps and quality gates.

## Roles

| Role | Purpose | Stage |
|------|---------|-------|
| [security-rule-expert](roles/security-rule-expert.md) | Design security vulnerability rules (OWASP/CWE) | Stage 1 (Parallel) |
| [performance-rule-expert](roles/performance-rule-expert.md) | Design performance anti-pattern rules | Stage 1 (Parallel) |
| [readability-rule-expert](roles/readability-rule-expert.md) | Design maintainability/readability rules | Stage 1 (Parallel) |
| [rule-architect](roles/rule-architect.md) | Integrate expert outputs into unified rule set | Stage 2 (Pipeline) |
| [test-validator](roles/test-validator.md) | Validate rules with test cases | Stage 3 (Pipeline) |
| [documentation-writer](roles/documentation-writer.md) | Produce documentation and adoption guide | Stage 4 (Pipeline) |

## Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Workflow definition with mermaid diagram, steps, gates |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | Resource constraints, behavioral constraints, failure handling |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | Skill and tool dependencies per role |
| [roles/*.md](roles/) | Role definitions (6 files) |

## Usage

Invoke this team when:
- Organization needs custom linting rules for a specific language/framework
- Existing linting tools don't cover organization-specific patterns
- Standardizing code quality across large engineering teams
- Building a gradual rollout linting system (not a blocking gate)

Do NOT use for:
- Manual per-PR code reviews (use a PR review team instead)
- One-off code quality checks (use a single-agent code review skill)
- Security audits of existing code (use a security audit team instead)

skills.name: event-planning-team
skills.short_desc:
4角色活动策划团队（创意策划师 + 预算规划师 + 可行性审核员 + 执行协调员），负责从 0 到 1 落地活动方案。
Use when 举办行业研讨会、发布会、团建等需要完整策划的活动。
Do NOT use for 简单行政杂事、单一环节执行、已有方案的微调。
skills.detail_desc:
# 活动策划团队

这是一个 Mixed (B+C) 模式的 Teamskill：并行分解阶段（创意策划师 + 预算规划师并行产出）+ 流水线阶段（可行性审核员整合审核 → 执行协调员落地计划）。解决单一代理在活动策划中的收敛性问题：创意忽略预算限制、预算忽略创意价值、执行忽略可行性风险。

## Workflow

0. **Pre-flight: 检查依赖与解析输入** — Leader 检查 [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml)，解析用户输入（活动类型、目标受众、活动目标、预期规模、时间框架、预算上限）。必填项缺失时请求用户补充。用户决定是否继续。

1. **并行创意与预算规划** — creative-planner 和 budget-planner 并行执行（隔离，互不可见）。创意策划师产出主题概念、内容模块、体验亮点、3 个创意选项；预算规划师产出预算明细、各选项评估、ROI 分析、优化建议。详见 [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 1。

2. **可行性审核** — feasibility-reviewer 整合 Step 1 输出，分析创意-预算匹配度，识别风险点，给出方案推荐，标注需用户决策的分歧点。Verdict 为 APPROVED/APPROVED-WITH-CAVEATS 时进入 Step 3；NEEDS-REVISION 返回 Step 1；BLOCKED 暂停等待用户决策。详见 [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 2。

3. **执行计划制定** — execution-coordinator 基于审核通过的方案，使用倒推法制定执行时间表，编制供应商清单，分解任务，提供风险预案。详见 [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 3。

4. **Final: 整合输出活动策划报告** — Leader 整合所有角色输出，生成最终活动策划报告，标注用户需确认事项。分歧点不消化，保留给用户决策。详见 [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 4。

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| creative-planner | 设计活动创意方案 | Step 1 并行阶段 | 活动类型、目标受众、活动目标、预期规模、时间框架、品牌背景 | 无 | [roles/creative-planner.md](roles/creative-planner.md) |
| budget-planner | 编制活动预算明细 | Step 1 并行阶段 | 活动类型、活动规模、时间框架、预算上限 | 无 | [roles/budget-planner.md](roles/budget-planner.md) |
| feasibility-reviewer | 审核创意-预算匹配度 | Step 2 串行阶段 | creative-planner 输出 + budget-planner 输出 + 活动目标 + 时间框架 | 无 | [roles/feasibility-reviewer.md](roles/feasibility-reviewer.md) |
| execution-coordinator | 制定执行计划 | Step 3 串行阶段 | 可行性审核员推荐方案 + 预算明细 + 活动日期 + 可用资源 | 无 | [roles/execution-coordinator.md](roles/execution-coordinator.md) |

> Before dispatching each teammate, read the corresponding role file and extract the
> `## Inline Persona for Teammate` section — paste it directly into the dispatch prompt.
> Most adopting agents do NOT auto-load role files for teammates.

## Files

| File | What it contains | When to read |
|---|---|---|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Mermaid 流程图、详细步骤、质量门控、Final Report 格式 | 首次派发前 — 完整执行协议 |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | 资源限制、行为约束、失败处理和降级模式 | 处理限制、失败或降级场景 |
| [roles/*.md](roles/) | 各角色身份、成功标准、输出格式、Inline Persona | 派发每个 teammate 前 — 提取 Inline Persona |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | 外部技能和工具依赖 | **启动时** — 检查依赖，报告缺失项，用户决定是否继续 |

skills.detail_desc:
# 田野调研团队

该团队以“职责分离 + 质量门控”的方式协同完成复杂研究任务，避免单角色在关键判断上同时当运动员和裁判。每个角色只对本阶段产出负责，由 Leader 最终整合为可执行决策包。

## Roles

| id | 职责 | 何时派发 | 输入 | 关键依赖 | 角色文件 |

| sampling-strategist | 制定样本分层与招募路径。 | 阶段 2 | 阶段 1 输出 | research-add-fields, python | [roles/sampling-strategist.md](roles/sampling-strategist.md) |

skills.name: travel-guide-team
skills.short_desc:
WHAT: 7-role deep travel guide creation pipeline (4 parallel researchers + curator + writer + editor) covering food, culture, accommodation, and entertainment with Lonely Planet-style narrative depth.
Use when creating深度游手册 or travel media content requiring comprehensive destination coverage beyond simple itinerary routes.
Do NOT use for simple行程路线图, single-attraction guides, or quick travel tips.
skills.detail_desc:
# Travel Guide Team

A 7-role deep travel guide creation pipeline following Lonely Planet editorial standards. Combines parallel research breadth (4 angle researchers) with sequential editorial depth (curator → writer → editor). Solves the single-agent failure mode where guides become either shallow lists or unstructured prose dumps.

**Pattern**: B+C (Parallel decomposition → Specialization pipeline)
- Stage 1: 4 parallel researchers work on disjoint slices (food / culture / accommodation / entertainment)
- Stage 2-4: Sequential editorial pipeline with quality gates

## Workflow

0. **Pre-flight: check dependencies** — read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and verify.
   Report missing items: `required: true` = likely fails without it; `required: false` = degraded but functional. **User decides** whether to proceed.
   The team can run on inline-persona-only mode if all skills are missing.
   Note: dependencies were populated during authoring via Stage 2 auto-matching (local scan) and optional post-generation community enrichment. Each entry's `source` field indicates origin (`local` or community URL).

1. **Parallel Research Phase** — Leader dispatches 4 researchers simultaneously.
   Each researcher investigates their assigned slice: food-researcher (culinary), culture-researcher (cultural), accommodation-researcher (lodging), entertainment-researcher (nightlife/leisure).
   See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 1 for quality gate (coverage check).

2. **Content Curation Phase** — content-curator integrates all research outputs.
   Creates hero narrative, content outline, flags gaps. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 2 for quality gate (structure ready check).

3. **Guide Writing Phase** — guide-writer produces full prose content.
   Writes Lonely Planet-style prose for all outlined sections. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 3 for quality gate (content complete check).

4. **Layout & Polish Phase** — layout-editor formats final guide.
   Applies visual hierarchy, quick-reference formatting, consistent style. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 4 for quality gate (layout complete check).

5. **Final: emit Travel Guide Report** — Leader assembles final guide with coverage map and creation metadata.
   See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 5 for report format.

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| food-researcher | Research culinary soul—dishes, eateries, food stories | Step 1 (parallel) | destination, travel_context | none | [roles/food-researcher.md](roles/food-researcher.md) |
| culture-researcher | Research cultural DNA—sites, traditions, etiquette | Step 1 (parallel) | destination, travel_context | none | [roles/culture-researcher.md](roles/culture-researcher.md) |
| accommodation-researcher | Research lodging—neighborhoods, booking, value | Step 1 (parallel) | destination, travel_context | none | [roles/accommodation-researcher.md](roles/accommodation-researcher.md) |
| entertainment-researcher | Research nightlife/leisure—scenes, safety, dress codes | Step 1 (parallel) | destination, travel_context | none | [roles/entertainment-researcher.md](roles/entertainment-researcher.md) |
| content-curator | Integrate research into outline—hero narrative, gaps | Step 2 (serial) | all research outputs | none | [roles/content-curator.md](roles/content-curator.md) |
| guide-writer | Write full prose—Lonely Planet style, hooks, practical | Step 3 (serial) | outline + research outputs | none | [roles/guide-writer.md](roles/guide-writer.md) |
| layout-editor | Format and polish—visual hierarchy, quick-reference | Step 4 (serial) | guide content + outline | none | [roles/layout-editor.md](roles/layout-editor.md) |

skills.name: newsletter-growth-team
skills.short_desc:
WHAT: 4-role specialization pipeline (Topic Strategist + Content Editor + Layout Designer + Growth Analyst) that produces subscriber-ready newsletters with enforced quality gates.
WHEN: Planning email newsletters, building subscriber engagement, or establishing private-domain content strategy.
Do NOT use for spam campaigns, one-off promotional emails, or non-email content formats.
skills.detail_desc:
# Newsletter Growth Team

A 4-role specialization pipeline that transforms newsletter ideas into subscriber-ready content packages. Each stage has strict handoff gates — the Topic Strategist's selection is immutable for downstream stages, the Content Editor's text is immutable for Layout and Growth roles. This prevents the common failure mode where newsletters have great content but stagnant growth, or beautiful layouts but weak messaging.

## Workflow

0. **Pre-flight: check dependencies** — read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and verify.
   Report missing items: `required: true` = likely fails without it; `required: false` = degraded but functional. **User decides** whether to proceed.
   The team can run on inline-persona-only mode if all skills are missing.
   Note: dependencies were populated during authoring via Stage 2 auto-matching (local scan) and optional post-generation community enrichment. Each entry's `source` field indicates origin (`local` or community URL).

1. **Topic Discovery** — topic-strategist receives newsletter context + research scope, produces ranked topic candidates with relevance scores and evidence. Gate: ≥3 topics with score ≥0.7, verdict = READY-FOR-EDITOR. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 1 for full gate criteria.

2. **Content Drafting** — content-editor receives approved topics + brand voice guide, produces full article drafts with refined headlines and actionable takeaways. Gate: all topics have ≥300-word drafts with hook → context → insight → takeaway structure, verdict = READY-FOR-LAYOUT. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 2.

3. **Visual Layout** — layout-designer receives article drafts + visual guide, produces newsletter layout structure with visual hierarchy, breakpoints, and designated CTA slots. Gate: all articles have ≥1 breakpoint per 200 words, CTA slots designated, mobile optimization validated, verdict = READY-FOR-GROWTH. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 3.

4. **Growth Optimization** — growth-analyst receives layout structure + newsletter context + historical metrics, populates CTA slots with growth-optimized copy, designs referral mechanics, provides subject line options. Gate: all CTAs populated, ≥1 referral mechanic, ≥3 subject lines, growth hypothesis stated, verdict = READY-FOR-SEND. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 4.

5. **Final: emit Newsletter Ready Report** — Leader integrates all stage outputs into a cohesive newsletter package, validates gate passes, surfaces unresolved issues. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 5 for report format.

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| topic-strategist | Discover and rank topics by audience relevance | Stage 1 (first) | Newsletter context, research scope | web-research (optional) | [roles/topic-strategist.md](roles/topic-strategist.md) |
| content-editor | Transform topics into polished article drafts | Stage 2 (after topic gate passes) | Approved topics, brand voice guide | none | [roles/content-editor.md](roles/content-editor.md) |
| layout-designer | Structure newsletter layout with visual hierarchy | Stage 3 (after content gate passes) | Article drafts, visual guide | none | [roles/layout-designer.md](roles/layout-designer.md) |
| growth-analyst | Populate CTAs and design growth mechanics | Stage 4 (after layout gate passes) | Layout structure, newsletter context, metrics | web-research (optional) | [roles/growth-analyst.md](roles/growth-analyst.md) |

skills.short_desc:
WHAT: 多机位剪辑专家组。负责同步、视角切换与叙事平衡。
WHEN: 处理大型演出、体育赛事或多机位访谈录制。
Do NOT use for 手机单机位拍摄。
skills.detail_desc:
# 多机位剪辑专家组

一个 3 角色专业化流水线（C 模式），将多机位素材转化为完整的剪辑蓝图。团队解决了单一代理在处理多机位剪辑时缺乏同步纪律、视角切换逻辑和叙事平衡的失败模式——每个阶段强制执行严格的交接契约，防止质量退化。

## Workflow

0. **Pre-flight: check dependencies** — 读取 [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) 并验证。报告缺失项目：`required: true` = 缺失时可能失败；`required: false` = 降级但仍可运行。**User decides** 是否继续。如果所有技能缺失，团队可以在仅使用内嵌角色提示词模式下运行。

1. **同步协调** — sync-coordinator 接收素材信息 + 可选同步要求。完成时间码对齐，识别关键同步点，标记素材质量问题。参见 [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 1 的完整协议和质量门控。

2. **视角切换设计** — angle-switcher 接收 sync-coordinator 输出 + 可选视角偏好。设计视角切换方案，定义机位使用场景，提供切换节奏建议。参见 [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 2 的完整协议和质量门控。

3. **叙事平衡调整** — narrative-balancer 接收 sync-coordinator + angle-switcher 输出 + 可选叙事目标。定义叙事结构，调整切换方案，提供观众体验旅程。参见 [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 3 的完整协议和质量门控。

4. **Final: emit 多机位剪辑蓝图** — Leader 整合所有输出为完整蓝图，包含同步基础、视角切换方案、叙事结构和实施建议。参见 [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 4 的最终报告格式。

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| sync-coordinator | 完成时间码对齐和质量评估 | Stage 1 (every run) | 素材信息 + 同步要求 | none | [roles/sync-coordinator.md](roles/sync-coordinator.md) |
| angle-switcher | 设计视角切换方案 | Stage 2 (after sync gate passes) | sync-coordinator 输出 + 视角偏好 | none | [roles/angle-switcher.md](roles/angle-switcher.md) |
| narrative-balancer | 调整叙事平衡 | Stage 3 (after angle gate passes) | sync-coordinator + angle-switcher 输出 + 叙事目标 | none | [roles/narrative-balancer.md](roles/narrative-balancer.md) |

skills.name: user-persona-generator-team
skills.display_name: 用户画像生成团队技能
skills.short_desc:
采用 4 角色协作构建业务用户画像：证据整理、分群与聚类解读、刻板印象纠偏与叙事定稿。
用于产品定义与增长策略前，将访谈与行为数据收敛为可行动人格。
不适用于无行为与访谈依据的拍脑袋用户口号。
skills.detail_desc:
# 用户画像建模团队

以「证据—分群—伦理—叙事」四段门控，减少单角色把个人偏好写成用户真相的风险。Leader 最终输出可放入 PRD/增长方案的人格包。

## Workflow

该 Teamskill 为严格的 **C 型串行门控流水线**（4 角色固定顺序）。先建立可追溯证据池，再做分群与标签，再做偏见/敏感项审计，最后才允许叙事化人格卡定稿，避免“先画人设再补证据”。

0. **前置检查：验证依赖** — 读取 [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml)，记录可用能力与缺失影响，并确认隐私边界可满足（必要时降级为更粗粒度聚合）。

1. **Leader 收集业务约束** — 产品阶段、目标市场、可用数据资产（访谈/问卷/埋点/CRM）、成功指标与隐私合规要求（PII/敏感属性边界）。

2. **evidence-curator 建立证据池** — 去标识化处理，输出 EvID 索引、样本范围说明、已支持命题与证据缺口清单，为分群提供可用字段/维度候选。

3. **cluster-segment-lead 产出可运营分群** — 给出 2–5 个分群定义（规则优先，聚类需解释特征与稳定性），并映射到可触达路径与产品杠杆；不可运营者标记 research-only。

4. **stereotype-guard 做偏见与可验证性审计** — 标出刻板印象/敏感标签/伪因果风险，给出可落地替代表述与保留条件（需法务/DEI 背书时显式占位）。

5. **persona-narrative-integrator 定稿人格卡** — 将结论绑定 EvID，输出人格卡、场景故事（标注戏剧化程度）、指标启示与误用警示（不可用于歧视性决策）。

6. **Leader 输出最终报告** — 输出 **用户画像决策包**（分群总览 + 人格卡 + 风险与行动清单）。

## Roles

| id | 职责 | 阶段 | 角色文件 |
|---|---|---|---|
| evidence-curator | 证据整理 | 1 | [roles/evidence-curator.md](roles/evidence-curator.md) |
| cluster-segment-lead | 分群建模叙事 | 2 | [roles/cluster-segment-lead.md](roles/cluster-segment-lead.md) |
| stereotype-guard | 偏见审查 | 3 | [roles/stereotype-guard.md](roles/stereotype-guard.md) |
| persona-narrative-integrator | 定稿整合 | 4 | [roles/persona-narrative-integrator.md](roles/persona-narrative-integrator.md) |

> 派发每位队友前复制对应角色文件中的 `## Inline Persona for Teammate`。

## Files

| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | 流程与门控 |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | 约束 |
| [roles/*.md](roles/) | 角色 |

skills.name: cross-functional-collaboration-team
skills.short_desc:
Conflict resolution and communication framework design for siloed teams via 5-role C+A pattern.
Use when launching initiatives that require Marketing, Engineering, and Sales to act as one.
Do NOT use for standalone department meetings or single-function planning.
skills.detail_desc:
# Cross-Functional Collaboration Team

A 5-role Mixed C+A pattern team that surfaces adversarial department perspectives (Marketing, Engineering, Sales) in isolation, diagnoses organizational dysfunctions using Lencioni's framework, and designs actionable conflict resolution protocols. Solves the failure mode where single-agent analysis converges toward one department's bias and misses the root tensions beneath surface disagreements.

## Workflow

0. **Pre-flight: check dependencies** — read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and verify. Report missing items to user who decides go/no-go. This team can run on inline-persona-only mode (no external skills required).

1. **Department Perspective Surfacing** — Leader dispatches marketing-advocate, engineering-advocate, and sales-advocate in parallel isolation. Each advocate surfaces their department's concerns, cross-departmental conflicts, and needs from others. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 1 for quality gate (≥2 concerns per advocate).

2. **Dysfunction Diagnosis** — dysfunction-diagnostician receives all three advocate outputs, maps tensions to Lencioni's Five Dysfunctions (Absence of Trust → Fear of Conflict → Lack of Commitment → Avoidance of Accountability → Inattention to Results), identifies root dysfunction, and prioritizes intervention order. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 2 for quality gate.

3. **Framework Design** — framework-designer receives diagnosis and advocate outputs, designs concrete mechanisms for each dysfunction, creates communication protocol and implementation roadmap. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 3 for quality gate.

4. **Final: emit Cross-Functional Collaboration Report** — Leader integrates all outputs, surfaces advocate contradictions verbatim (never mediates), provides implementation roadmap. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) for final report format.

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| marketing-advocate | Surface Marketing's perspective on brand, messaging, timing | Step 1 (parallel) | Initiative context, stakeholders | none | [roles/marketing-advocate.md](roles/marketing-advocate.md) |
| engineering-advocate | Surface Engineering's perspective on feasibility, stability | Step 1 (parallel) | Initiative context, stakeholders | none | [roles/engineering-advocate.md](roles/engineering-advocate.md) |
| sales-advocate | Surface Sales' perspective on revenue, deal timelines | Step 1 (parallel) | Initiative context, stakeholders | none | [roles/sales-advocate.md](roles/sales-advocate.md) |
| dysfunction-diagnostician | Diagnose dysfunctions using Lencioni's framework | Step 2 (serial) | All advocate outputs | none | [roles/dysfunction-diagnostician.md](roles/dysfunction-diagnostician.md) |
| framework-designer | Design conflict resolution and communication framework | Step 3 (serial) | Diagnosis + advocate outputs | none | [roles/framework-designer.md](roles/framework-designer.md) |

> Before dispatching each teammate, read the corresponding role file and extract the
> `## Inline Persona for Teammate` section — paste it directly into the dispatch prompt.
> Most adopting agents do NOT auto-load role files for teammates.

## Files

skills.name: technical-review-team
skills.short_desc:
WHAT: 3-role specialization pipeline (SME + Copy Editor + Fact Checker) for verifying complex technical content before mass publication.
WHEN: Reviewing technical manuscripts, documentation, or educational content requiring precision across accuracy, prose, and citations.
NOT: For creative fiction review, non-technical content, or single-aspect reviews (use single-agent skills instead).
skills.detail_desc:
# Technical Review Team

This team implements a **specialization pipeline (Pattern C)** inspired by O'Reilly's technical book review process. It solves the failure mode where a single reviewer cannot simultaneously maintain technical precision, prose quality, and citation integrity — blurring these boundaries causes regressions (e.g., the editor rewriting technical explanations, the fact checker evaluating code correctness).

## Workflow

0. **Pre-flight: check dependencies** — read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and verify.
   Report missing items: `required: true` = likely fails without it; `required: false` = degraded but functional. **User decides** whether to proceed.
   The team can run on inline-persona-only mode if all skills are missing.

1. **Stage 1: SME Technical Review** — subject-matter-expert / content + domain context / verify technical accuracy, code correctness, terminology.
   See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 1 for the full protocol. Gate: PASS → proceed; NEEDS-REVISION → revision loop (max 2 retries); BLOCK → halt.

2. **Stage 2: Copy Editor Review** — copy-editor / SME-approved content + SME output / review prose clarity, consistency, readability.
   See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 2 for the full protocol. Gate: PASS → proceed; NEEDS-EDITS → integrate edits; BLOCK → halt.

3. **Stage 3: Fact Checker Verification** — fact-checker / SME + Copy Editor approved content / verify citations, statistics, external claims.
   See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 3 for the full protocol. Gate: PASS → proceed; NEEDS-CORRECTION → correction loop (max 2 retries); BLOCK → halt.

4. **Final: emit Technical Review Report** — Leader integrates all findings, validates gates, compiles publication-ready verdict.
   See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 4 for the Final Report format.

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| subject-matter-expert | Verify technical accuracy, code correctness, terminology precision | Stage 1 (first) | Content + domain context | none | [roles/subject-matter-expert.md](roles/subject-matter-expert.md) |
| copy-editor | Review prose clarity, consistency, readability | Stage 2 (after SME gate passes) | SME-approved content + SME output | none | [roles/copy-editor.md](roles/copy-editor.md) |
| fact-checker | Verify citations, statistics, external claims | Stage 3 (after Copy Editor gate passes) | SME + Copy Editor approved content + prior outputs | fact-checker skill (optional) | [roles/fact-checker.md](roles/fact-checker.md) |

> Before dispatching each teammate, read the corresponding role file and extract the
> `## Inline Persona for Teammate` section — paste it directly into the dispatch prompt.

skills.name: ar-filter-creator-team
skills.display_name: AR 滤镜设计团队技能
skills.short_desc:
AR 滤镜设计组（创意师+技术美术+交互专家）。
Use when 制作抖音、Ins 上的互动营销滤镜。

skills.detail_desc:
# ar-filter-creator-team

AR 滤镜设计组。由创意总监、技术美术、交互设计师组成的三人专家团队，专门负责制作抖音、Instagram 上的互动营销滤镜，将品牌创意转化为可交互的 AR 体验。

## Workflow

0. **Pre-check: Validate Dependencies** — Read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and validate.
   Report missing items: `required: true` = may fail without this; `required: false` = degraded but functional. **User decides** whether to continue.
   This team has no external dependencies — all roles operate on inline role knowledge.

1. **Parallel Review Stage** — Leader dispatches AR Creative Director, Technical Artist, and Interaction Designer simultaneously. Each role works independently. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Stage 1.

2. **Leader Integration Stage** — Leader integrates all three outputs, cross-validates alignment, marks agreements and conflicts. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Stage 2.

3. **Final: Output AR Filter Design Report** — Leader outputs comprehensive AR filter design report with creative concept, technical specs, and interaction flow. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) § Final Report Format.

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| ar-creative-director | 定义创意概念 | Stage 1, parallel | `{BRAND_MESSAGE}`, `{TARGET_AUDIENCE}`, `{CAMPAIGN_GOAL}` | none | [roles/ar-creative-director.md](roles/ar-creative-director.md) |
| technical-artist | 实现技术方案 | Stage 1, parallel | `{CREATIVE_CONCEPT}`, `{PLATFORM_SPEC}`, `{PERFORMANCE_TARGET}` | none | [roles/technical-artist.md](roles/technical-artist.md) |
| interaction-designer | 设计交互流程 | Stage 1, parallel | `{CREATIVE_CONCEPT}`, `{USER_BEHAVIOR}`, `{FACE_TRACKING_CAPS}` | none | [roles/interaction-designer.md](roles/interaction-designer.md) |

> Before dispatching each teammate, read the corresponding role file and extract
> the `## Inline Persona for Teammate` section — paste it directly into the dispatch prompt.
> Most agent implementations won't auto-load role files.

## Files

| File | What it contains | When to read |
|---|---|---|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Mermaid diagram, detailed steps, integration rules, final report format | Before first dispatch — complete execution manual |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | Resource constraints, behavioral constraints, failure handling, degradation modes | When encountering limits, handling failures, or needing degradation rules |
| [roles/*.md](roles/) | Each role's identity, success criteria, output format, inline persona | Before dispatching each teammate — extract Inline Persona |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | Required external skills and tools | **At startup** — validate dependencies, report missing, user decides |

## WHAT

AR 滤镜设计要素：
- **创意概念**: 视觉风格、叙事主题、品牌植入方式
- **技术实现**: 3D 模型、材质贴图、粒子特效、性能优化
- **交互设计**: 触发机制、手势交互、面部追踪响应

## WHEN

Use for:
- 制作抖音/Instagram 互动营销滤镜
- 品牌活动 AR 体验设计
- 节日主题滤镜创作
- 产品推广 AR 互动
- 社交媒体病毒式传播滤镜

## NOT

Do NOT use for:
- 简单的图片修图（非 AR 项目）
- 静态图片滤镜设计
- 非互动型视觉效果
- 纯 2D 图像处理

## Inspiration

TikTok Effect House 官方指南: https://effecthouse.tiktok.com/learn

核心原则：
1. 创意优先 — 滤镜要有独特的视觉吸引力和叙事性
2. 交互驱动 — 用户参与是滤镜成功的关键
3. 性能优化 — 移动端流畅运行是基础要求
4. 品牌融合 — 自然植入品牌元素，不破坏用户体验

## Usage

```markdown
请使用 ar-filter-creator-team 设计以下 AR 滤镜：

[品牌信息]
- 品牌名称: [品牌]
- 营销目标: [推广产品/节日活动/品牌曝光]
- 目标受众: [年龄/兴趣/平台偏好]

[可选详情]
- 视觉风格偏好: [可爱/酷炫/梦幻/写实]
- 交互类型偏好: [面部追踪/手势触发/环境互动]
- 平台: [抖音/Instagram/其他]
- 性能要求: [低端设备兼容/高端效果优先]
```

## Output Example

```markdown
## AR 滤镜设计报告

### 1. 创意概念方案

**滤镜主题**: [品牌] 春日焕新魔法滤镜

**视觉风格定义**
| 风格维度 | 定义 | 参考元素 |
|---------|------|---------|
| 色调 | 柔和粉彩 + 品牌主色点缀 |樱花粉、天空蓝、品牌绿 |
| 光效 | 柔光晕染 + 粒子飘散 | 光斑、花瓣粒子 |
| 3D 元素 | 品牌产品微缩模型 | 产品 3D 模型悬浮 |
| 整体氛围 | 梦幻、轻盈、治愈 | 日系动漫风格参考 |

**叙事主题**
- 开场: 用户面部被柔光包裹，花瓣开始飘落
- 发展: 品牌产品 3D 模型从画面边缘飞入
- 高潮: 用户眨眼触发产品特效爆发
- 结尾: 品牌 Logo 淡入定格

**品牌植入方式**
| 植入元素 | 出现时机 | 持续时间 | 植入方式 |
|---------|---------|---------|---------|
| 产品 3D 模型 | 开场 2 秒后 | 全程 | 悬浮环绕用户面部 |
| 品牌 Logo | 结尾定格 | 3 秒 | 淡入叠加 |
| 品牌色粒子 | 全程 | 持续 | 飘散粒子带品牌色 |

### 2. 技术实现方案

**3D 模型规格**
| 模型名称 | 面数上限 | 贴图尺寸 | 动画类型 |
|---------|---------|---------|---------|
| 产品模型 A | 5,000 面 | 512x512 | 旋转 + 悬浮 |
| 花瓣粒子 | 100 面/个 | 256x256 | 飘落 + 旋转 |
| 光晕效果 | 程序生成 | N/A | 脉动 + 扩散 |

**材质与贴图**
| 材质类型 | 着色器 | 特效参数 |
|---------|--------|---------|
| 产品材质 | PBR Simplified | 金属度 0.3, 光泽度 0.7 |
| 花瓣材质 | Unlit Transparent | 透明度渐变 0.8→0 |
| 光晕材质 | Additive Blend | 颜色脉冲 1.5 秒周期 |

**粒子系统**
| 粒子类型 | 最大数量 | 生命周期 | 运动模式 |
|---------|---------|---------|---------|
| 花瓣粒子 | 50 个 | 5 秒 | 随机飘落 + 风力影响 |
| 光斑粒子 | 30 个 | 3 秒 | 环绕面部 + 脉动 |
| 品牌色粒子 | 20 个 | 4 秒 | 跟随产品模型 |

**性能优化方案**
| 优化项 | 目标值 | 实现方式 |
|---------|--------|---------|
| 帧率 | ≥30 FPS (低端设备) | LOD 分级、粒子数量动态调整 |
| 内存占用 | ≤50MB | 模型压缩、贴图合并 |
| 启动时间 | ≤2 秒 | 预加载关键资源 |

### 3. 交互设计方案

**触发机制定义**
| 触发类型 | 触发条件 | 响应效果 | 用户提示 |
|---------|---------|---------|---------|
| 面部检测 | 检测到面部 | 滤镜激活，花瓣开始飘落 | 无（自动） |
| 眨眼触发 | 检测到眨眼动作 | 产品特效爆发，粒子激增 | "眨眼试试！" |
| 张嘴触发 | 检测到张嘴 | 品牌产品放大展示 | "张嘴看惊喜！" |
| 头部转动 | 头部角度变化 | 3D 模型跟随视角调整 | 无（自动） |

**手势交互设计**
| 手势类型 | 检测方式 | 触发效果 | 适用场景 |
|---------|---------|---------|---------|
| 挥手 | 手部追踪 | 粒子方向改变 | 增强互动感 |
| 指向 | 手部追踪 | 产品模型移动到指向位置 | 产品展示 |
| 双手合十 | 手部追踪 | 特殊特效触发 | 节日主题 |

**面部追踪响应**
| 迱踪点 | 追踪精度 | 响应元素 | 响应方式 |
|---------|---------|---------|---------|
| 面部轮廓 | 高精度 | 光晕边界 | 紧贴面部边缘 |
| 眼睛位置 | 中精度 | 眼部特效 | 眨眼触发检测 |
| 嘴巴位置 | 中精度 | 嘴部特效 | 张嘴触发检测 |
| 头部角度 | 高精度 | 3D 模型位置 | 视角跟随调整 |

### 4. 跨维度验证

- ✅ 创意概念与技术实现匹配（模型面数支持创意效果）
- ✅ 交互设计与性能目标兼容（触发检测不影响帧率）
- ⚠ 部分特效需进一步性能测试验证

### 5. 实施建议

| 优先级 | 项目 | 输出物 | 时间节点 |
|--------|------|--------|---------|
| P0 | 3D 模型制作 | 产品模型 + 粒子模型 | 第 1 周 |
| P1 | 材质与贴图 | PBR 材质库 | 第 2 周 |
| P2 | 交互逻辑实现 | 触发检测 + 响应脚本 | 第 3 周 |
| P3 | 性能优化测试 | 多设备测试报告 | 第 4 周 |
| P4 | 最终调优 | 发布版本 | 第 5 周 |
```

skills.name: effective-feedback-coach-team
skills.short_desc: Training team for Non-Violent Communication (NVC) and Radical Candor frameworks. Use when improving team morale and performance through structured peer reviews. NOT for formal disciplinary actions.
skills.detail_desc:
# Effective Feedback Coach Team

A specialized team for training and coaching teams on effective feedback methodologies, combining Non-Violent Communication (NVC) and Radical Candor frameworks to improve team morale, performance, and psychological safety.

## Core Frameworks

### Non-Violent Communication (NVC)
Developed by Marshall Rosenberg, NVC provides a structured approach to communication that fosters compassion and understanding. The core OFNR model:
- **Observation**: State facts without evaluation or judgment
- **Feeling**: Express emotions clearly and authentically
- **Need**: Identify underlying universal human needs
- **Request**: Make clear, actionable requests

### Radical Candor
Developed by Kim Scott, Radical Candor combines personal caring with direct challenging to create a culture of honest, growth-oriented feedback:
- **Care Personally**: Show genuine concern for team members as human beings
- **Challenge Directly**: Provide honest, specific feedback to help people grow
- Avoid: Obnoxious Aggression (challenge without care), Ruinous Empathy (care without challenge), and Manipulative Insincerity (neither care nor challenge)

## When to Use This Skill

**Appropriate Use Cases:**
- Teams struggling with giving or receiving feedback constructively
- Improving psychological safety and trust within teams
- Training managers on effective 1:1 feedback conversations
- Designing peer review processes for performance improvement
- Resolving communication breakdowns between team members
- Building a feedback-positive culture during team formation or restructuring
- Preparing for performance review cycles
- Onboarding new team leads on feedback best practices

**NOT Appropriate For:**
- Formal disciplinary actions or PIP documentation
- Termination conversations
- Legal or HR-mandated investigations
- Conflict resolution requiring mediation (use dedicated mediation resources)
- Performance issues requiring immediate intervention

## Workflow

```mermaid
graph LR
  A[Leader: Pre-flight] --> B{Input OK?}
  B -- No --> C[Degraded Mode]
  B -- Yes --> D[NVC Trainer: Foundation]
  D --> E[Radical Candor Coach: Framework]
  E --> F[Peer Review Facilitator: Practice]
  F --> G[Leader: Integrate]
  G --> H[Final Report]
```

C-pattern team with sequential pipeline execution.

## Roles

| id | Purpose | Role file |
|---|---|---|
| nvc-trainer | Train team on Non-Violent Communication (NVC) techniques using the OFNR model | [roles/nvc-trainer.md](roles/nvc-trainer.md) |
| radical-candor-coach | Coach on Radical Candor feedback framework - Care Personally, Challenge Directly | [roles/radical-candor-coach.md](roles/radical-candor-coach.md) |
| peer-review-facilitator | Facilitate structured peer review sessions with actionable outcomes | [roles/peer-review-facilitator.md](roles/peer-review-facilitator.md) |

## Files

| File | When to read |
|---|---|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Before first dispatch |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | When hitting limits |
| [roles/*.md](roles/) | Before dispatching |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | Startup |

## Expected Inputs

When invoking this skill, provide:
1. **Context**: Team size, structure, and current feedback culture
2. **Challenge**: Specific feedback challenges or situations being faced
3. **Goals**: Desired outcomes (e.g., "improve 360 review quality", "reduce defensive reactions")
4. **Constraints**: Time available, cultural considerations, organizational policies

## Expected Outputs

The team will deliver:
1. **NVC Assessment**: Analysis of current communication patterns with specific improvement areas
2. **Radical Candor Guidance**: Personalized coaching on balancing care and challenge
3. **Peer Review Framework**: Structured session design with templates and facilitation guides
4. **Integrated Action Plan**: Combined recommendations with prioritized next steps

## References

- Kim Scott, "Radical Candor: Be a Kick-Ass Boss Without Losing Your Humanity" (2017)
- Marshall B. Rosenberg, "Nonviolent Communication: A Language of Life" (2003)
- https://www.radicalcandor.com/
- Center for Nonviolent Communication: https://www.cnvc.org/

skills.name: expense-audit-team
skills.short_desc:
费用合规性审计组，通过三角度对抗性审查确保票据合规、成本合理、流程高效。
用于提交复杂差旅报销或季度清算。
不适用于日常零钱记账或简单报销。
skills.detail_desc:
# 费用合规性审计组 (Expense Audit Team)

这是一个 **A-pattern (Adversarial / Cross-check)** 团队，解决单一代理在报销审查时的"合规盲点"问题。三个角色从不同角度独立审查报销：票据验证师检查票据合规，成本优化师检查成本合理性，流程审计师检查流程效率。Leader 整合所有输出，发现矛盾时原样呈现，不做调解。

## Workflow

0. **Pre-flight: check dependencies** — read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and verify.

1. **Parallel Adversarial Audit** — Three roles run simultaneously in isolation:
   - receipt-validator: 验证票据合规性和真实性
   - cost-optimizer: 识别成本优化机会和预算影响
   - process-auditor: 分析流程效率和风险

2. **Final: emit 费用审计评估报告** — Leader 整合三个角度的审查结果。

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| receipt-validator | 验证票据合规性 | 每次运行（并行） | 票据信息 + 报销政策 | [roles/receipt-validator.md](roles/receipt-validator.md) |
| cost-optimizer | 识别成本优化机会 | 每次运行（并行） | 报销明细 + 市场价格 | [roles/cost-optimizer.md](roles/cost-optimizer.md) |
| process-auditor | 分析流程效率 | 每次运行（并行） | 流程信息 + 权限设置 | [roles/process-auditor.md](roles/process-auditor.md) |

## Files

| File | What it contains | When to read |
|---|---|---|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Mermaid 图、对抗性审查步骤、整合规则 | Before first dispatch |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | 资源限制、行为约束、失败处理 | When hitting limits |
| [roles/*.md](roles/) | 每个角色的身份、成功标准、Inline Persona | Before dispatching |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | 外部依赖 | **Startup** |

skills.name: smart-contract-legal-audit-team
skills.display_name: 智能合约法律审计团队
skills.short_desc:
3角色团队(Legal Compliance Officer, Code-as-Law Analyst, Jurisdiction Specialist)：法律合规阶段审查智能合约法律可执行性,代码即法律分析阶段评估代码即法律风险  辖地专家阶段确定管辖权适用性
Use when 发布高价值DeFi或NFT协议前需要法律审计 Do Not use for 纯solidity代码审计
skills.detail_desc:
# Smart Contract Legal Audit Team

A 3-role specialization pipeline for auditing Smart Contracts against legal enforceability and jurisdictional code-as-law risks. It addresses the failure mode where technical security audits miss legal risks — unenforceable terms, regulatory violations, and jurisdictional conflicts that can invalidate protocol operations or expose parties to liability.

## Workflow

0. **Pre-flight: check dependencies** — read [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) and verify.
   Report missing items: `required: true` = likely fails without it; `required: false` = degraded but functional. **User decides** whether to proceed.
   The team can run on inline-persona-only mode if all skills are missing.

1. **Legal Compliance Assessment** — legal-compliance-officer receives smart contract code and protocol documentation, produces legal enforceability analysis with regulatory compliance mapping. Gate: assessment covers all mandatory legal domains (contract formation, consumer protection, securities law, data privacy). See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 1.

2. **Code-as-Law Analysis** — code-as-law-analyst receives the legal compliance assessment + smart contract code, produces code-as-law mapping report identifying where code execution creates binding obligations. Gate: all critical contract functions mapped to legal effects. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 2.

3. **Jurisdiction Evaluation** — jurisdiction-specialist receives the code-as-law mapping + protocol operational scope, produces jurisdiction-specific compliance matrix with conflict-of-law analysis. Gate: all target jurisdictions assessed with risk ratings. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 3.

4. **Final: emit Smart Contract Legal Audit Report** — Leader integrates all stage outputs into a structured report. See [workflow.md](workflow.md) Step 4.

## Roles

| id | Purpose | When dispatched | Input | Key dependencies | Role file |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| legal-compliance-officer | Assess legal enforceability and regulatory compliance | Every run, Stage 1 | Smart contract code + protocol documentation | None | [roles/legal-compliance-officer.md](roles/legal-compliance-officer.md) |
| code-as-law-analyst | Map code execution to binding legal obligations | Every run, Stage 2 (after legal compliance gate passes) | Legal compliance assessment + smart contract code | None | [roles/code-as-law-analyst.md](roles/code-as-law-analyst.md) |
| jurisdiction-specialist | Evaluate jurisdiction-specific requirements and conflicts | Every run, Stage 3 (after code-as-law gate passes) | Code-as-law mapping + protocol operational scope | None | [roles/jurisdiction-specialist.md](roles/jurisdiction-specialist.md) |

> Before dispatching each teammate, read the corresponding role file and extract the
> `## Inline Persona for Teammate` section — paste it directly into the dispatch prompt.
> Most adopting agents do NOT auto-load role files for teammates.

## Files

| File | What it contains | When to read |
|---|---|---|
| [workflow.md](workflow.md) | Mermaid diagram, step-by-step protocol, quality gates, Final Report format | Before first dispatch — the complete playbook |
| [bind.md](bind.md) | Resource limits, behavioral constraints, failure handling and degraded modes | When hitting limits, handling failures, or needing degraded-mode rules |
| [roles/*.md](roles/) | Per-role identity, success criteria, output schema, Inline Persona for Teammate | Before dispatching each teammate — extract Inline Persona |
| [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml) | External skills and tools required to run | **Startup** — verify deps, report missing items, user decides go/no-go |

skills.name: clinical-protocol-team
skills.display_name: 临床研究方案团队技能
skills.short_desc:
采用 5 角色协作草拟临床研究方案：问题澄清、试验设计、统计规划、伦理审查与综合评估。
用于多学科会前快速形成可评审方案草稿并暴露高风险设计点。
不替代伦理委员会、临床专家和统计师的正式审查。
skills.detail_desc:
# 临床方案团队

该团队以“职责分离 + 质量门控”的方式协同完成复杂研究任务，避免单角色在关键判断上同时当运动员和裁判。每个角色只对本阶段产出负责，由 Leader 最终整合为可执行决策包。

## Workflow

0. **前置检查：验证依赖** — 读取 [dependencies.yaml](dependencies.yaml)，确认依赖可用并标注缺失影响。
1. **clinical-question-lead**：澄清研究问题、终点与纳排标准。
2. **trial-designer**：设计对照结构、随机化与执行流程。
3. **biostat-planner**：规划样本量与统计分析路径。
4. **ethics-risk-reviewer**：审查受试者风险与伦理敏感项。
5. **protocol-synthesizer**：整合方案分歧并输出评审稿。
6. **Leader 组装最终交付** — 输出 **临床方案评审包**，包含结论、证据、风险与行动项。

## Roles

| id | 职责 | 何时派发 | 输入 | 关键依赖 | 角色文件 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| clinical-question-lead | 澄清研究问题、终点与纳排标准。 | 阶段 1 | 任务简报 | clinical-trial-protocol-skill, python | [roles/clinical-question-lead.md](roles/clinical-question-lead.md) |
| trial-designer | 设计对照结构、随机化与执行流程。 | 阶段 2 | 阶段 1 输出 | clinical-trial-protocol-skill, python | [roles/trial-designer.md](roles/trial-designer.md) |
| biostat-planner | 规划样本量与统计分析路径。 | 阶段 3 | 阶段 2 输出 | clinical-trial-protocol-skill, python | [roles/biostat-planner.md](roles/biostat-planner.md) |
| ethics-risk-reviewer | 审查受试者风险与伦理敏感项。 | 阶段 4 | 阶段 3 输出 | clinical-trial-protocol-skill, python | [roles/ethics-risk-reviewer.md](roles/ethics-risk-reviewer.md) |
